    Asus ROG Zephyrus M16 (2023)
    Best Asus laptop
    $1500 at Best Buy (RTX 4070)
  • Acer Chromebook Vero 514
    Best laptop under $500
    $500 at Best Buy
  • Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
    Best Samsung laptop
    $1264 at Best Buy (14 inches)

The top laptops available at Best Buy in 2023

Apple MacBook Pro (2023)
The best overall laptop at Best Buy

For those who need the best possible laptop

$1799 $1999 Save $200

The best possible laptop you can buy at Best Buy is the Apple MacBook Pro (2023). This laptop comes in 14-inch and 16-inch sizes, and comes with the M2 Pro or M2 Max chips, ideal for video and photo editing, and for those who own other Apple devices.

Pros
  • The Apple M2 chip is powerful
  • Great for those who own other Apple devices
  • A great display and design
Cons
  • Not for those who like Windows
  • Screen has a notch
$1799 at Best Buy (14 inches) $2299 at Best Buy (16 inches)

The best possible laptop you can find at Best Buy is also one of the most popular. It's the Apple MacBook Pro (2023). Though this laptop has a high cost, it packs plenty of value for the money. The laptop comes in both 14-inch and 16-inch sizes, for one, has an Arm-based SoC under the hood, built and designed by Apple. Even the display is great, though there's a notch. Of course, it's not for those who like Windows, though, as there's a learning curve for macOS.

Anyway, under the hood of this laptop is the Apple M2 Pro or Apple M2 Max CPU. These CPUs are crazy good, thanks to the power of Apple's integrated GPU. On 14-inch models, you can choose either a 10-core CPU with a 16-core GPU on the M2 Pro, a 12-core CPU with a 19-core CPU, or all the way up to a 12-core CPU and a crazy 30-core GPU. 16-inch models have 12-core CPUS, and up to a 38-core GPU. And since this is an Arm-based chip, you get superb performance on battery, and when plugged into power for tasks like video editing or photo editing (unlike Windows laptops.)

What else is good about these laptops is the design. You can choose either Silver or Space Gray, but these laptops are crazy thin and light for the power they offer. Of course, ports are limited (2x Thunderbolt, SD card reader, and HDMI), but the MacBook is 0.66 inches thick, and weighs about 4.7 pounds. The display is great, too, since these are Liquid Retina XDR displays that pack in incredible contact ratios and lots of pixels (3024x1964 on 14-inch models and 3456x2234 on 16-inch models).

Razer Blade 16
Best gaming laptop

Powerful specs to play the latest games

The Razer Blade 16 is packing powerful 55W Intel processor and Nvidia GeForce RTX 40 series graphics with up to 175W of power. It even has the option for a dual-mode mini-LED display.

Pros
  • It has a 24-core CPU and up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 with 175W if power
  • Great display for both gaming and content creation
  • 1080p webcam with Windows Hello is rare for gaming laptops
Cons
  • Very expensive
  • Somewhat bulky
$3000 at Best Buy

It's not in everyone's price range, but the best gaming laptop you can find at Best Buy right now is without a doubt the Razer Blade 16. This powerful machine comes with the latest and greatest specs, plus it's Razer's first 16-inch laptop.

The Razer Blade 16 comes with an Intel Core i9-13950HX, an incredibly powerful 55W processor with 24 cores and 32 threads. That gives you near desktop-level CPU performance and a core count that would be hard to imagine on a laptop just a couple of years ago. You can also go up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 Laptop GPU, giving you all the performance you could want for gaming. In addition, the laptop comes with up to 32GB of DDR5 RAM, which you can upgrade later, along with up to 2TB of SSD storage.

As Razer's first 16-inch laptop, the Blade 16 comes with a 16:10 aspect ratio and Quad HD+ resolution plus a super-smooth 240Hz refresh rate. That's the base model, but the RTX 4080 and RTX 4090 models have a dual-mode mini-IPS display, which can actually change its native resolution and refresh rate, from Ultra HD+ at 60Hz for creative work to Full HD+ at 240Hz for gaming. There's also a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello facial recognition, a rarity for gaming laptops, even premium ones.

Razer also delivers on the design front, with a very clean look that could almost fit in any environment if it weren't for the glowing Razer logo on the lid. It does have an RGB keyboard, though. The laptop starts at 5.4 pounds of weight, so it's not super light, but it makes sense for these specs. Ending with ports, the Blade 16 comes with two USB-C ports (one with Thunderbolt 4), three USB Type-A ports, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a full-size SD card reader.

The Razer Blade 16 isn't a cheap laptop, but it delivers the good on just about every front.

HP Spectre x360 13.5 (2023)
Best HP laptop

Featuring a premium design, a 3:2 display, and high-end specs, the Spectre x360 13.5 is one of the best convertibles and laptops overall you can buy today.

Pros
  • A convertible form factor is more versatile than a typical laptop
  • Tall 3:2 display with optional OLED configuration
  • One of the best webcams on any laptop
Cons
  • Best Buy doesn't have all the configurations HP offers
  • It's not terribly light considering its size
$1500 at Best Buy $1750 at Best Buy (OLED)

It shouldn't come as a surprise that HP's Spectre laptops are fantastic, and the Spectre x360 13.5 is easily one of the best the company has ever made.

Performance-wise, it's packing 13th-generation processors from Intel, specifically an Intel Core i7-1355U. This is a very capable processor with 10 cores and 12 threads, and it can handle day-to-day tasks without any issues. In addition, this model comes with 16GB of RAM and an up to 1TB SSD, which is very much a high-end combination. Of course, it's not a gaming laptop, but it's great for everyday use.

As the name suggests, the HP Spectre x360 13.5 has a 13.5-inch screen, and it comes in the 3:2 aspect ratio, even taller than the 16:10 format many companies use. The base configuration comes in Full HD+ resolution, but higher-end models have a stellar 3K2K OLED panel, which is super sharp and gives you benefits like true blacks and more vibrant colors, making for a stunning visual experience for both work and media consumption. HP also uses one of the best webcams you can find on a laptop — a 5MP sensor with Windows Hello support.

The latest Spectre x360 models don't have the same bold design of years past, but you still get a premium dual-tone look in Nightfall Black and Nocturne Blue, with the former being especially striking. You can also opt for the more boring Natural Silver if you want to. The laptop comes in at 3.01 pounds, but that makes sense for a convertible laptop made of aluminum. You get a solid selection of ports, too, with two Thunderbolt 4 connections, USB Type-A, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader.

There haven't been many changes with the latest model compared to last year's, but it was already great, as we noted in our review. The added performance is just a nice bonus.

Dell XPS 13 Plus
Best Dell laptop
$1303 $1650 Save $347

The Dell XPS 13 Plus is a futuristic-looking laptop with top-tier performance and stunning display options packed into one of the coolest designs we've seen for a laptop.

Pros
  • Powerful Intel P-series processors
  • Fantastic display, including a 3.5K OLED option
  • Futuristic and modern design
Cons
  • That new design may take some getting used to
  • Only two Thunderbolt 4 ports
$1303 at Best Buy FHD $1700 at Best Buy 3.5K OLED $1950 at Best Buy 13th

The Dell XPS lineup includes some of the best laptops on the market, and the Dell XPS 13 Plus is arguably the cream of the crop.

There is a version of the XPS 13 Plus with 13th-generation Intel processors, but it's not yet available at Best Buy. The 12th-generation models come with up to an Intel Core i7-1260P, which gives you 12 cores and 16 threads, and the 13th-gen versions (with a Core i7-1360P) are pretty similar, only slightly faster. You're not missing out on much by going with last year's model if you can't wait, considering everything else is the same. The laptop also includes up to 32GB of RAM and a 1TB SSD.

The display options available are also great, with this being a 13.4-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio. The base model uses an IPS panel at Full HD+ resolution, but you have the option for a stunning 3.5K OLED panel, which gives you true blacks and vivid colors that are impossible to mimic on an IPS panel. A downside of this laptop is that it does come with a 720p webcam, something Dell refuses to move on from even on its premium laptops. At least it has Windows Hello facial recognition.

The Dell XPS 13 Plus still looks futuristic and very unique, thanks to a few design choices. First, the touchpad is invisible and seamless with the chassis of the laptop. Dell also removed the physical function keys, replacing them with touch-based buttons, and opted for a zero-lattice keyboard. It's very portable, too, weighing 2.77 pounds. A potential problem is the port selection, which includes just two Thunderbolt 4 ports. Dell includes some adapters for a headphone jack and USB Type-A to help with that, though you may end up needing a dock.

Even with that downside, the Dell XPS 13 Plus is an undeniably cool laptop with top-tier performance and a great display. Whether you buy the 12th-gen model or wait for the newer one, you'll have a great experience.

MacBook Air (M2)
The best Apple laptop

For students or just fans of macOS

$899 $1099 Save $200

The MacBook Air M2 is one of the best Apple laptops you can find at Best Buy. It has a fresh new design and more powerful performance while being just as efficient as the previous model, making it one of the best clamshell laptops around.

Pros
  • Top-tier performance and efficiency courtesy of the Apple M2 chip
  • Great display included in every configuration
  • Premium build quality in four different colors
Cons
  • Storage and RAM upgrades are expensive
  • Base storage configuration is slower than the others
$899 at Best Buy (13 inches) $1299 at Best Buy (15 inches)

It's hard to talk about the best laptops on the market without mentioning the MacBook Air, and with the latest iteration, Apple has made one of the most popular laptops in the world even better, making it our pick for the best Apple laptop at Best Buy.

The latest MacBook Air is powered by the Apple M2 chip, an 8-core Arm-based CPU that promises up to 18% more CPU performance and up to 35% more GPU performance compared to its predecessor. Apple Silicon is extremely efficient compared to Intel or AMD processors, too, so battery life will be fantastic here. At Best Buy, you can choose between an 8-core or 10-core GPU, and all the models come with 8GB of RAM and up to 512GB SSD. Springing for the higher-end model may be worth it mostly because of the SSD, which is much faster in the 512GB model.

Apple has stretched the screen compared to previous models, so it's slightly taller, and it's now 13.6 inches diagonally with a 2560x1664 resolution, which is very sharp for this size. There's even a 15-inch model now, that packs more pixels with a 2880x1864 resolution. That display does have a notch, though, housing the upgraded 1080p webcam, backed by the image signal processor in the Apple M2 to further enhance quality.

This iteration of the MacBook Air comes in a brand-new chassis that's more balanced and still incredibly light, starting at just 2.7 pounds. It also comes in new colors, Starlight and Midnight, in addition to Silver and Space Gray. As for ports, we get two USB4/Thunderbolt connections, a headphone jack with support for high-impedance headphones, and a MagSafe connector for fast charging. It's not a ton of ports, but you can use a dock or hub if you need more.

Aside from that shortcoming, this is a great laptop in almost every way and one we can easily recommend to most people.

Lenovo Yoga 9i (2023)
Best Lenovo laptop

The best laptop on the market

The 2023 Lenovo Yoga 9i adds 13th-generation Intel Core processors to one of the best laptops on the market, with stunning OLED displays and a premium, shiny design.

Pros
  • 13th-generation Intel Core i7
  • Stunning OLED display, with two versions available
  • Striking metal design with polished edges
Cons
  • Can't store the included pen
$1400 at Best Buy (2.8K OLED) $1650 at Best Buy (4K OLED)

If you've read our review of the Lenovo Yoga 9i, it should come as no surprise that it's featured here. It's arguably the best laptop on the market, blending versatility, performance, and a fantastic media experience.

Starting with performance, the 2023 Lenovo Yoga 9i is powered by a 13th-generation Intel Core i7-1360P, a 12-core, 16-thread processor that should handle all kinds of day-to-day tasks without any issues. It also comes with 16GB of RAM and up to a 1TB SSD, everything you need for a great experience.

The display is another highlight, being a 14-inch panel OLED with a 16:10 aspect ratio. There are two configurations available — one with 2.8K resolution and a 90Hz refresh rate and another with 4K resolution and a 60Hz refresh rate, so you can choose what you value more. I'd argue the 2.8K model makes more sense for most people, as 4K won't be that much sharper at this size, and there's more of a benefit with the extra smoothness of 90Hz. This laptop also includes a 1080p webcam by default, including Windows Hello support.

The Lenovo Yoga 9i is one of the most beautiful laptops on the market. It comes in Oatmeal and Storm Grey colorways, and both look fantastic, with shiny polished edges and smooth metal surfaces. It also weighs 3.09 pounds, so it's fairly portable for a 14-inch convertible made of aluminum. Coming around to the ports, there are two Thunderbolt 4 connections, one standard USB Type-C port alongside one USB Type-A, and a headphone jack. Compared to most premium ultrabooks of this size, that's not bad at all.

The Lenovo Yoga 9i is a fantastic laptop in almost every way and a very easy one to recommend. The 2023 model only really upgrades the performance, but nothing else needed to change for it to be great.

Source: Asus
Asus ROG Zephyrus M16 (2023)
Best Asus laptop

A great gaming laptop that's still decently light

$1500 $1950 Save $450

The Asus ROG Zephyrus M16 is a powerful gaming laptop with 13th-gen Intel Core processors and Nvidia GeForce RTX 40 series graphics. It also has a great display, and it's decently light for what it offers.

Pros
  • Powerful 13th-gen Intel processors and Nvidia RTX graphics
  • Quad HD+ 240Hz display, including a mini-LED option
  • Relatively light for what it offers
Cons
  • Only the RTX 4070 model is currently available
  • It can get a bit expensive
$1500 at Best Buy (RTX 4070) $2700 at Best Buy (RTX 4080) $3300 at Best Buy (RTX 4090)

Asus makes some of the best gaming laptops on the market, and if you're shopping around at Best Buy, the ROG Zephyrus M16 is one of the best you can buy.

Starting with performance, the Asus ROG Zephyrus M16 comes with a 13th-generation Intel Core i9-13900H processor, with 14 cores and 20 threads delivering excellent performance for gaming and day-to-day use. It also comes with up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 Laptop GPU, though only the RTX 4070 model is currently available in new condition at Best Buy. Still, with 140W of power, you're getting a ton of performance here. You also get 16GB of RAM (which is upgradeable) and a 1TB SSD.

Things also look great in the display department, which a 16-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio, Quad HD+ resolution, and a smooth 240Hz refresh rate. All models also include 100% coverage of DCI-P3, but while the RTX 4070 model comes with a standard IPS panel, the higher-end versions have a mini-LED panel with hundreds of dimming zones for an even better viewing experience. And above that display is a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello support, which is great to see.

For the power it offers, the Asus ROG Zephyrus M16 is fairly light, starting at 4.63 pounds, and it looks very sleek, too. It's an all-black chassis, though there's a set of LED lights on the lid that can be customized with monochrome images and animations. The keyboard also supports single-zone RGB lighting. As for ports, we're looking at two USB-C ports (one supporting Thunderbolt 4), two USB Type-A ports, HDMI, and a 3.5mm headphone jack.

While it's a bit much for most people, the Asus ROG Zephyrus M16 is a great laptop with a lot of performance and a great display. If you're into gaming, you can't go wrong here.

Acer Chromebook Vero 514
Best laptop under $500

A solid experience for very cheap

The Acer Chromebook Vero 514 offers solid entry-level performance and a decent display in an eco-friendly design that looks different from anything else on the market.

Pros
  • A sustainable and eco-friendly design
  • Very capable specs for the price
  • Decent supply of ports
Cons
  • Chrome OS may not be good enough for everyone
  • Not as fancy or portable as more premium laptops
$500 at Best Buy

We can't all afford to spend nearly $1,000 on a new laptop, and the Acer Chromebook Vero 514 is a fantastic option if you're looking for something affordable. Yes, it's a Chromebook, so it might not be ideal if you're accustomed to Windows laptops, but for most people, this laptop can do anything you need it to do.

Performance-wise, the Acer Chromebook Vero 514 is powered by 12th-generation Intel Core processors, specifically a Core i3-1215U. That gives you six cores and eight threads for solid performance in all kinds of day-to-day tasks, whether you're browsing the web, watching videos, or even playing some lighter games. It also comes with 8GB of RAM and a 128GB SSD for storage, which is quite good for a Chromebook.

The display is also not too shabby. This is a 14-inch IPS panel with a typical 16:9 aspect ratio, which is still the most prevalent format in this price range. It comes in Full HD resolution, so it's a pretty sharp screen for its size, and it should make for a good visual experience overall. Above that display is a 1080p webcam, a rare sight for such an affordable laptop, but a welcome one, no doubt.

As part of Acer's Vero lineup, the Chromebook Pro 514 has a very environmentally-conscious design philosophy. The chassis uses unpainted recycled plastic materials, and while it's certainly not for everyone, this rough texture look definitely helps it stand out from the crowd. The laptop weighs in at 3.09 pounds, so it's not particularly light for its size, but it's still decently portable. It also comes with a decent selection of ports. You get two USB Type-C ports, one USB Type-A, HDMI, and a headphone jack, so all the basics are covered and all your peripherals should work out of the box without needing adapters.

All of that comes in at just under $500, and that's not including any discounts, which can make this an even better deal. At this price range, you can't ask for much more.

Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
Best Samsung laptop

A premium clamshell laptop with a stunning AMOLED display

$1264 $1450 Save $186

The Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is a fantastic premium laptop with 13th-gen Intel processors, a sturdy design, and a beautiful AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate.

Pros
  • Stunning AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate
  • Solid port selection for a premium laptop
  • 13th-generation Intel Core processors
Cons
  • A bit pricy
  • HDMI port only supports 4K at 30Hz
$1264 at Best Buy (14 inches) $1450 at Best Buy (16 inches)

Samsung is primarily known for its smartphones and the surrounding peripherals, but the company has also started making some of the best laptops you can buy each year. With the Galaxy Book 3 Pro, that's more true than ever.

For starters, the Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro features Intel's 13th-generation Intel Core processors, specifically with a Core i7-1360P. This 28W processor has 12 cores and 16 threads, delivering fantastic performance in day-to-day tasks. That processor is backed up by 16GB of RAM and up to 1TB of SSD storage, making for a great experience overall.

The real highlight, however, is the display. The laptop comes in both 14-inch and 16-inch flavors, but both feature a super-sharp 16:10 Dynamic AMOLED 2X panel. The resolution is 2880x1800, which looks fantastic in either size, and you also get a smooth 120Hz refresh rate that makes motion and animations look a lot nicer. Plus the usual benefits of OLED, like deep blacks and vibrant colors. There's a 1080p camera above that display delivering solid video quality, though Windows Hello is only supported via a fingerprint reader.

This iteration also comes with an all-aluminum design that feels very premium and solid. The laptop is still incredibly thin, but it's a lot heavier now. The 14-inch version comes in at 2.58 pounds, while the 16-inch model weighs 3.4 pounds. That's the trade-off for using aluminum, which feels more premium than the magnesium of previous models. Arguably one of the highlights of this laptop is the port selection, which includes two Thunderbolt 4 ports, one USB Type-A port, HDMI, and a headphone jack. The HDMI port is limited to 4K at 30Hz, but it's nice to have the option, at least.

With a fantastic display, high-end performance, and a premium design, the Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is one of the best laptops you can buy right now.

Best laptops at Best Buy in 2023: Bottom line

That gives you a few fantastic options if you're shopping for a laptop at Best Buy. All of these are high-end, premium devices that will give you a great experience. Arguably the best one of the bunch is the MacBook Pro. This laptop packs in the efficiency of the Apple M2 silicon under the hood, and it has a great design. It also comes with different screen sizes.

Apple MacBook Pro (2023)
The best overall laptop at Best Buy

For those who need the best possible laptop

$1799 $1999 Save $200

The best possible laptop you can buy at Best Buy is the Apple MacBook Pro (2023). This laptop comes in 14-inch and 16-inch sizes, and comes with the M2 Pro or M2 Max chips, ideal for video and photo editing, and for those who own other Apple devices.

$1799 at Best Buy (14 inches) $2299 at Best Buy (16 inches)

If you can't afford to spend that much, the Acer Chromebook Vero 514 is a great option for those that don't need Windows. It runs ChromeOS, and the specs it has are more than good enough to give you a great experience for day-to-day use, such as browsing the web and writing up documents. It all costs under $500, so it's great if you have limited funds.

With that being said, all of these are great laptops with a lot to give. In the case of the Windows laptops, they're all shipping with Windows 11 and you can get future updates, such as version 22H2, for free, so they'll last you a while longer. If you're not finding what you're looking for here, check out the best AMD Ryzen laptops, since the company didn't make many appearances on this list.

FAQ

Q: What is the return policy for laptops at Best Buy?

Of course, if you're shopping at Best Buy, it's important to know that you can count on a good return policy in case something goers wrong. The return policy at Best Buy gives you some room to breathe.

Most products are returnable within a 15-day period after the purchase for all customers. If you have a My Best Buy Plus or My Best Buy Total membership, that period is extended up to 60 days, which gives you even more time to find potential defects or realize the laptop you got isn't for you.

You can return the laptop you bought to a retail location or mail it back to Best Buy for free for a full refund, but the condition is that the laptop must be in like-new condition. If the laptop is visibly damaged, unsanitary, or missing any of the included accessories, Best Buy may deny the return. A return may also be denied if you're missing proof of purchase, or some fees, such as state taxes, may not be refunded if you return the product without proof of purchase.

Finally, "final sale" items are non-returnable, so if you bought an item on clearance, you may not be able to return it.

item.title: Best gaming mice in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best gaming mice in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Buying a good gaming mouse is one of the best investments you can make for your gaming setup, alongside the best mechanical keyboard. A top-notch gaming mouse isn't going to magically make you a better gamer, but it'll greatly improve your overall experience. Whether you're upgrading to a better sensor or simply ditching wires to remove cable clutter, there are plenty of options to choose from. Here are some of our favorite pointers.

  • Source: Razer
    Razer DeathAdder V3 Pro
    Best overall
    $147 at Amazon
  • Logitech G502 X Plus
    Best wireless
    $134 at Amazon
  • Source: Logitech
    Logitech G203 Lightsync
    Best budget wired
    $30 at Amazon
  • Source: Logitech
    Logitech G305 Lightspeed
    Best budget wireless
    $40 at Amazon
  • Source: Glorious
    Glorious Model O Gaming Mouse
    Best lightweight
    $35 at Amazon
  • Source: Razer
    Razer Naga V2 Pro
    Best MOBA/MMO
    $180 at Amazon

Our top picks for gaming mice in 2023

Source: Razer
Razer DeathAdder V3 Pro
Best overall

The undisputed king of gaming mice

$147 $150 Save $3

The Razer DeathAdder V3 Pro is about as good as you can get for a gaming mouse. It has Razer's impressive Focus Pro 30K optical sensor, optical mouse switches, up to 90 hours of battery life, and is relatively large without weighing too much. It's an absolute joy to use.

Pros
  • Excellent sensor
  • Solid build quality
  • Reliable wireless
Cons
  • A little on the bulky side
$147 at Amazon $150 at Best Buy $150 at Razer

If ever there was a gaming mouse that transcended all others it would be Razer's DeathAdder. It's one of the most iconic, best-selling mice of all time, and also one of the most comfortable. The latest version, the DeathAdder V3 Pro, is the biggest update to this legendary product in many years, and it still retains everything that made it so special to begin with.

The shape is mostly still the same, and it's still supremely ergonomic and comfortable to use. Alas, it's still a right-handed only design. But as with recent other gaming mice, Razer has been using the expertise of professional gamers to help make it better. As such, the DeathAdder V3 Pro is 25% lighter than its predecessor without the need to cut holes all over the mouse. It also gets Razer's latest and greatest tech, including support for its 4000Hz HyperPolling wireless dongle, the latest 30,000 DPI optical sensor, and Gen 3 optical switches.

Razer's magic comes not just from the hardware, though. Through its Synapse application, you're able to calibrate the mouse to the type of surface you're using it on, as well as the lift-off and landing distances. It's now also more power-efficient than ever, promising up to 90 hours of life when used wirelessly. Or you can plug it in using Razer's excellent Speedflex cable, which is so light that it doesn't even feel like it's there. DPI stages and a custom profile can also be stored on the mouse, so you can move between machines without needing to use Synapse on them both. As long as the DeathAdder exists, it's going to be the gaming mouse to beat.

Logitech G502 X Plus
Best wireless

Cut the wires with Logitech's flagship gaming mouse

Logitech packed the G502 X Plus full of premium features. There's a 25,000 DPI-capable optical sensor, optical switches for the buttons, plenty of internal space for profiles, programmable buttons, tasteful RGB, and killer wireless connectivity.

Pros
  • Reliable wireless
  • Excellent sensor
  • Plenty of programmable buttons
Cons
  • A little on the bulky side
$134 at Amazon $140 at Newegg

Logitech's G502 X Plus is one incredible wireless gaming mouse. The specification sheet is full of premium entries, including the excellent HERO optical sensor with a maximum supported DPI setting of 25,000. It's capable of hitting up to 400 IPS and handling 40G of acceleration, making it ideal for flinging across a large mousepad with quick reflex gameplay. If you're seeking one of the best wireless gaming pointers, look no further than this puppy.

It's not lightweight by any stretch of the imagination, but this hefty weight makes the mouse feel all the more comfortable to use as a daily driver. And with a battery life of up to 130 hours (that's measured with the RGB lighting disabled), you won't be tied to the PC case for long. Gamers enjoy using Logitech mice because of the Lightspeed technology, and it's no joke. This wireless technology is super reliable and lightning fast, hence the name.

Just like other Logitech gaming mice, the G502 X Plus can be configured using the brand's software package to set profiles, manage programmable buttons, RGB lighting effects, and much more. It's a close second when compared to the Razer DeathAdder V3 Pro and can be considered a contender for the best wireless gaming mouse crown.

Source: Logitech
Logitech G203 Lightsync
Best budget wired

Save money with Logitech's excellent value mouse

$30 $40 Save $10

The Logitech G203 Lightsync is a reliable, wired gaming mouse that's easy for us to recommend for budget shoppers. Despite the affordable price tag, it offers a good set of features including a good sensor, a comfortable form factor, and more.

Pros
  • Great sensor
  • Amazing value
  • Good selection of colors
Cons
  • Not the lightest mouse
$30 at Amazon $40 at Newegg

If you're looking for a top-quality budget gaming mouse, then it can't get better than the Logitech G203 Lightsync. The Lightsync name indicates this mouse's compatibility with the Logitech G app and RGB lighting system. This means you can use the supported software to sync the lighting of the mouse with other Logitech peripherals. For a budget gaming mouse, it's actually fairly heavy on the RGB, with customizable zones you can tweak to your heart's desire. The Logitech G203 Lightsync has six programmable buttons in total and a DPI range of 200-8,000.

The sensor used in this mouse is tried and tested and is suitable for consistent performance across a variety of games. The G203 is smaller than many gaming mice, so it's more suitable for people with small hands. All in all, we think the Logitech G203 Lightsync sits comfortably in the category of budget-conscious gaming mice. There's no shortage of affordable gaming mice out there, but we think the G203 Lightsync is better than others in more ways than one. It has a no-nonsense design, good RGB support, a reliable sensor, and a comfortable form factor. There are only a handful of gaming mice out there that offer those features, especially for this price.

Source: Logitech
Logitech G305 Lightspeed
Best budget wireless

You'll find it difficult to beat this value

$40 $50 Save $10

The Logitech G305 Lightspeed is an affordable and reliable wireless gaming mouse. It's available in a variety of colors and has a comfortable form factor that makes it easy to use.

Pros
  • Reliable wireless
  • Good performance
  • Decent battery life
Cons
  • AA battery
  • Can feel a little too small
$40 at Amazon $40 at Newegg $50 at Best Buy

The Logitech G305 Lightspeed is an easy recommendation when it comes to the best wireless gaming mouse on a budget. It's a fairly simple-looking mouse that's available in a bunch of different, beautiful colors. It has an ambidextrous design, too, which is less common than it should be. Its small size makes it comfortable for both palm and claw grippers. Weighing in at 99g, it's not the lightest mouse out there, but it effortlessly glides and moves around on different surfaces, making it a solid option for FPS gamers. You will also appreciate the PTFE feet that come out of the box.

One thing that's worth pointing out about the Logitech G305 Lightspeed is that it uses a single AA battery. Depending on your outlook that's either great or a mild frustration. The battery life is pretty good though, with up to 250 hours of use from a single battery. The battery is what's partially responsible for some added weight. But it's not really a dealbreaker when you consider the performance you get for such a low price.

But if you're willing to look past that then you've got yourself a fantastic gaming mouse with a reliable and powerful sensor. It offers a max DPI of 12,000, which makes it better than a lot of gaming mice on the market. You can use the Logitech G Hub software to configure and save up to five profiles, thanks to the onboard storage. The Logitech G305 Lightspeed comes with six fully programmable buttons. In addition to the left and right-click buttons, you get two additional buttons on the side along with a clickable scroll wheel and a DPI button. It's a lot of function and performance for not a lot of money.

Source: Glorious
Glorious Model O Gaming Mouse
Best lightweight

Lift off to the next level with this accurate pointer

The Glorious Model O- is our pick for the best lightweight FPS gaming mouse you can buy in 2022. It only weighs 58g and offers plenty of great features including a reliable sensor, G-skates mouse feet, and more.

Pros
  • Excellent performance
  • Lightweight
  • Perforated design
Cons
  • Limited number of buttons
$35 at Amazon

If you're in the market to buy a good quality lightweight FPS gaming mouse, then we think the Glorious Model O- should definitely be on top of the list. The Glorious Model O- is essentially a smaller version of the Model O. This gaming mouse weighs only 58g, making it one of the lightest options out there. The mouse has a honeycomb shell which is key to achieving a feather-light weight. It's not going to magically turn you into a human aimbot, but it'll definitely allow you to explore lower DPI levels and work on your form.

One of the best things about this particular mouse is that it comes with G-skates feet. This eliminates the need to buy third-party skates for your mouse for smooth tracking. That, in addition to a reliable sensor, makes it a great option for FPS gaming. The Glorious Model O- uses a Pixart 3360 sensor and has a 1000Hz polling rate and lift-off distance of less than 0.7mm, which is among the best in the industry.

The Model O gaming mouse comes with a total of six buttons and uses Omron switches that are rated for over 20 million clicks. The mouse also has plenty of RGB lights on the body, which you can easily control via companion software. You can also use the same software to create custom profiles with different DPI settings to be changed on the fly. There's also a dedicated DPI indicator at the bottom of the mouse that makes it easier for you to identify the right setting without having to open the software each time to change the setting. A mouse full of holes might seem strange, but try this one, you'll love it.

Source: Razer
Razer Naga V2 Pro
Best MOBA/MMO

Customize your gaming mouse with swappable grips!

The Razer Naga V2 Pro is designed for those who frequently switch between game genres and don't want to have more than one pointer. The side grip can be swapped out for different configurations. One designed for MMOs, another for MOBAs, making this one versatile mouse.

Pros
  • Excellent sensor
  • Swappable grips
  • Supports MOBAs and MMOs
Cons
  • Expensive
$180 at Amazon $180 at Newegg $180 at Razer

The Razer Naga Trinity ruled the MMO/MOBA space for many years until it was recently dethroned by its successor, the Naga Pro. The new Razer Naga V2 Pro uses the Naga Trinity as a foundation and upgrades it by adding a few useful features. Like other modern gaming mice, the 100% PTFE feet allow it to glide smoothly across different surfaces. The Razer Naga V2 Pro also uses Razer's optical-mechanical switches instead of regular mechanical switches. Depending on the type of games you play, you can switch between three swappable plates to have either 2, 6, or 12 buttons on the side.

The Naga V2 Pro also uses Razer's Focus Pro optical sensor with a max DPI of 30,000. This wireless mouse uses Razer's HyperSpeed Wireless for reliable connection and lag-free tracking, making it a solid option for a variety of games. Razer says the mouse is good for 300 hours of usage on a single charge (Bluetooth), which is pretty good. You can also just plug in the mouse and continue using it if you ever run out of juice too. Razer also sells a sophisticated Mouse Dock for charging, but it's disappointing you don't get in the box with a premium mouse like this one. But if you're an MMO player, this is the mouse you need.

Choosing the best gaming mouse to buy

We still believe the Razer DeathAdder V3 Pro to be one of the greatest gaming mice created. I personally use it on my main PC, and it's exceptional in just about any PC game, aside from MOBAs and MMOs where more buttons are required. The Razer Focus Pro sensor is among the best in the business, and so too are its optical switches for the main clickers. It's a little pricey, especially when you can buy capable mice for less than $70, but it's well worth the asking price.

Source: Razer
Razer DeathAdder V3 Pro
Best overall
$147 $150 Save $3

The Razer DeathAdder V3 Pro is about as good as you can get for a gaming mouse. It has Razer's impressive Focus Pro 30K optical sensor, optical mouse switches, up to 90 hours of battery life, and is relatively large without weighing too much. It's an absolute joy to use.

$147 at Amazon $150 at Best Buy $150 at Razer
item.description:
Buying a good gaming mouse is one of the best investments you can make for your gaming setup, alongside the best mechanical keyboard. A top-notch gaming mouse isn't going to magically make you a better gamer, but it'll greatly improve your overall experience. Whether you're upgrading to a better sensor or simply ditching wires to remove cable clutter, there are plenty of options to choose from. Here are some of our favorite pointers.

  • Source: Razer
    Razer DeathAdder V3 Pro
    Best overall
    $147 at Amazon
  • Logitech G502 X Plus
    Best wireless
    $134 at Amazon
  • Source: Logitech
    Logitech G203 Lightsync
    Best budget wired
    $30 at Amazon
  • Source: Logitech
    Logitech G305 Lightspeed
    Best budget wireless
    $40 at Amazon
  • Source: Glorious
    Glorious Model O Gaming Mouse
    Best lightweight
    $35 at Amazon
  • Source: Razer
    Razer Naga V2 Pro
    Best MOBA/MMO
    $180 at Amazon

Our top picks for gaming mice in 2023

Source: Razer
Razer DeathAdder V3 Pro
Best overall

The undisputed king of gaming mice

$147 $150 Save $3

The Razer DeathAdder V3 Pro is about as good as you can get for a gaming mouse. It has Razer's impressive Focus Pro 30K optical sensor, optical mouse switches, up to 90 hours of battery life, and is relatively large without weighing too much. It's an absolute joy to use.

Pros
  • Excellent sensor
  • Solid build quality
  • Reliable wireless
Cons
  • A little on the bulky side
$147 at Amazon $150 at Best Buy $150 at Razer

If ever there was a gaming mouse that transcended all others it would be Razer's DeathAdder. It's one of the most iconic, best-selling mice of all time, and also one of the most comfortable. The latest version, the DeathAdder V3 Pro, is the biggest update to this legendary product in many years, and it still retains everything that made it so special to begin with.

The shape is mostly still the same, and it's still supremely ergonomic and comfortable to use. Alas, it's still a right-handed only design. But as with recent other gaming mice, Razer has been using the expertise of professional gamers to help make it better. As such, the DeathAdder V3 Pro is 25% lighter than its predecessor without the need to cut holes all over the mouse. It also gets Razer's latest and greatest tech, including support for its 4000Hz HyperPolling wireless dongle, the latest 30,000 DPI optical sensor, and Gen 3 optical switches.

Razer's magic comes not just from the hardware, though. Through its Synapse application, you're able to calibrate the mouse to the type of surface you're using it on, as well as the lift-off and landing distances. It's now also more power-efficient than ever, promising up to 90 hours of life when used wirelessly. Or you can plug it in using Razer's excellent Speedflex cable, which is so light that it doesn't even feel like it's there. DPI stages and a custom profile can also be stored on the mouse, so you can move between machines without needing to use Synapse on them both. As long as the DeathAdder exists, it's going to be the gaming mouse to beat.

Logitech G502 X Plus
Best wireless

Cut the wires with Logitech's flagship gaming mouse

Logitech packed the G502 X Plus full of premium features. There's a 25,000 DPI-capable optical sensor, optical switches for the buttons, plenty of internal space for profiles, programmable buttons, tasteful RGB, and killer wireless connectivity.

Pros
  • Reliable wireless
  • Excellent sensor
  • Plenty of programmable buttons
Cons
  • A little on the bulky side
$134 at Amazon $140 at Newegg

Logitech's G502 X Plus is one incredible wireless gaming mouse. The specification sheet is full of premium entries, including the excellent HERO optical sensor with a maximum supported DPI setting of 25,000. It's capable of hitting up to 400 IPS and handling 40G of acceleration, making it ideal for flinging across a large mousepad with quick reflex gameplay. If you're seeking one of the best wireless gaming pointers, look no further than this puppy.

It's not lightweight by any stretch of the imagination, but this hefty weight makes the mouse feel all the more comfortable to use as a daily driver. And with a battery life of up to 130 hours (that's measured with the RGB lighting disabled), you won't be tied to the PC case for long. Gamers enjoy using Logitech mice because of the Lightspeed technology, and it's no joke. This wireless technology is super reliable and lightning fast, hence the name.

Just like other Logitech gaming mice, the G502 X Plus can be configured using the brand's software package to set profiles, manage programmable buttons, RGB lighting effects, and much more. It's a close second when compared to the Razer DeathAdder V3 Pro and can be considered a contender for the best wireless gaming mouse crown.

Source: Logitech
Logitech G203 Lightsync
Best budget wired

Save money with Logitech's excellent value mouse

$30 $40 Save $10

The Logitech G203 Lightsync is a reliable, wired gaming mouse that's easy for us to recommend for budget shoppers. Despite the affordable price tag, it offers a good set of features including a good sensor, a comfortable form factor, and more.

Pros
  • Great sensor
  • Amazing value
  • Good selection of colors
Cons
  • Not the lightest mouse
$30 at Amazon $40 at Newegg

If you're looking for a top-quality budget gaming mouse, then it can't get better than the Logitech G203 Lightsync. The Lightsync name indicates this mouse's compatibility with the Logitech G app and RGB lighting system. This means you can use the supported software to sync the lighting of the mouse with other Logitech peripherals. For a budget gaming mouse, it's actually fairly heavy on the RGB, with customizable zones you can tweak to your heart's desire. The Logitech G203 Lightsync has six programmable buttons in total and a DPI range of 200-8,000.

The sensor used in this mouse is tried and tested and is suitable for consistent performance across a variety of games. The G203 is smaller than many gaming mice, so it's more suitable for people with small hands. All in all, we think the Logitech G203 Lightsync sits comfortably in the category of budget-conscious gaming mice. There's no shortage of affordable gaming mice out there, but we think the G203 Lightsync is better than others in more ways than one. It has a no-nonsense design, good RGB support, a reliable sensor, and a comfortable form factor. There are only a handful of gaming mice out there that offer those features, especially for this price.

Source: Logitech
Logitech G305 Lightspeed
Best budget wireless

You'll find it difficult to beat this value

$40 $50 Save $10

But if you're willing to look past that then you've got yourself a fantastic gaming mouse with a reliable and powerful sensor. It offers a max DPI of 12,000, which makes it better than a lot of gaming mice on the market. You can use the Logitech G Hub software to configure and save up to five profiles, thanks to the onboard storage. The Logitech G305 Lightspeed comes with six fully programmable buttons. In addition to the left and right-click buttons, you get two additional buttons on the side along with a clickable scroll wheel and a DPI button. Razer also sells a sophisticated Mouse Dock for charging, but it's disappointing you don't get in the box with a premium mouse like this one. But if you're an MMO player, this is the mouse you need.

Choosing the best gaming mouse to buy

We still believe the Razer DeathAdder V3 Pro to be one of the greatest gaming mice created. I personally use it on my main PC, and it's exceptional in just about any PC game, aside from MOBAs and MMOs where more buttons are required. The Razer Focus Pro sensor is among the best in the business, and so too are its optical switches for the main clickers. It's a little pricey, especially when you can buy capable mice for less than $70, but it's well worth the asking price.

Source: Razer
Razer DeathAdder V3 Pro
Best overall
$147 $150 Save $3

The Razer DeathAdder V3 Pro is about as good as you can get for a gaming mouse. It has Razer's impressive Focus Pro 30K optical sensor, optical mouse switches, up to 90 hours of battery life, and is relatively large without weighing too much. It's an absolute joy to use.

$147 at Amazon $150 at Best Buy $150 at Razer

item.title: Best Android apps and games for kids in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best Android apps and games for kids in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Are you looking for some great Android apps and games to keep your kids occupied? If so, you’ve come to the right place. We’ve selected some of the best Android apps that will help your kids learn new things while also having fun. There are also some cool games that will keep your kids entertained in their free time.

Best Educational Apps for Kids

Khan Academy Kids (Ages 2 to 8)

3 Images

Khan Academy is known for its free educational courses, but it also has an app for kids. The Khan Academy Kids app includes thousands of educational activities, songs, games, and books. Its content can help children learn reading, language, writing, math, and more. It's free to download and use, and doesn’t include any kind of ads or subscriptions.

Khan Academy Kids: Learning! Developer: Khan Academy
Price: Free
4.7
Download

Moose Math by Duck Duck Moose (Ages 3 to 7)

3 Images

Moose Math is a free learning game that teaches kids about numbers, counting, addition, subtraction, geometry, and measurement. It features five activities, like Pet Bingo and Moose Juice, to teach math. It is suitable for all ages but is aligned with Common Core State Standards for Kindergarten and 1st Grade. Moose Math also comes from the stable of Khan Academy, through its subsidiary Duck Duck Moose.

Moose Math by Duck Duck Moose Developer: Duck Duck Moose, LLC
Price: Free
4.3
Download

Kahoot! Numbers by DragonBox (Ages 4-8)

3 Images

Kahoot! Numbers by DragonBox is another math learning app that turns numbers into colorful characters, making arithmetic far more appealing to younger audiences. It includes various different activities, like Sandbox and Puzzle, to explore and learn basic math. Under these activities, there are hundreds of lessons, so there's plenty for your child to remain busy with. While the app requires a subscription, it offers a 7-day free trial for new users.

Read Along by Google (Ages 5 and up)

3 Images

Read Along is a free reading tutor app from Google. It helps children improve their reading skills in nine languages - English, Hindi, Bangla, Tamil, Telugu, Urdu, Marathi, Spanish, and Portuguese. The app also includes a digital assistant called Diya that encourages kids while reading and helps them when needed. The app can work without an internet connection, once it has been fully downloaded.

Read Along by Google Developer: Google LLC
Price: Free
Download

Epic (Ages 6 to 12)

3 Images

Epic is a digital reading app that includes kid-safe books, audiobooks, videos and more. There are over 40,000 books available on the app, including picture books, graphic novels, comics, non-fiction, and more. The language selection is also decent, with books in Spanish, Chinese, and French, apart from English.

Epic is free to download but requires a subscription to access the complete library. The Basic account is however free to use and gives access to one free book a day from a limited selection.

Epic: Kids' Books & Reading Developer: Epic! Creations Inc
Price: Free
4.3
Download

Duolingo ABC (Ages 3-8)

3 Images

Another great application from the minds of Duolingo is Duolingo ABC, which helps children learn to read and write in English. It offers over 700 personalized lessons, interactive stories, alphabet and phonics courses, sight words, multi-sensory activities, and a completely ad-free experience. Kids can also track their progress and customize their profiles with fun cartoon avatars. The app is available for free with no in-app purchases.

Learn to Read - Duolingo ABC Developer: Duolingo
Price: Free
4.1
Download

PBS Kids Games (Ages 2 to 8)

3 Images

PBS Kids Games apps pack hundreds of educational games that make learning fun. There are games about science, math, reading, art, and more. Additionally, these games feature popular characters like Daniel Tiger, Wild Kratts, Curious George, and more from PBS shows. Moreover, the games can be downloaded for offline use. The app is free to download and use, but it is only available in select markets.

PBS KIDS Games Developer: PBS KIDS
Price: Free
4.3
Download

Spelling & Phonics: Kids Games (Ages 3 and up)

3 Images

The Spelling & Phonics: Kids Games app includes over ten spelling games that will help your child learn to spell. It includes various modes, like Fill-in-the-Blank, Blank Spelling, and CVC for a fun learning experience. The app also packs a report card that will show your child’s progress. The app is free to download and use, and doesn’t include any ads or in-app purchases.

Spelling & Phonics: Kids Games Developer: RV AppStudios
Price: Free
4.6
Download

Kindergarten Kids Learning Games (Ages 2 to 6)

3 Images

The Kindergarten Kids Learning Games app comes with games to help your child learn alphabets, shapes, counting, body parts, and more. It features an attractive and colorful design to keep the children engaged. Additionally, the alphabet learning section has support for English, Hindi, and Spanish languages. There is a coloring book section as well in the app that has printable coloring pages for kids to have fun with. The app is free to download and use, but there are some in-app purchases and ads.

Kindergarten kid Learning Game Developer: Greysprings
Price: Free
4
Download

Women Who Changed the World (All ages)

3 Images

Do you want your kids to learn about some of the women who changed the world in a big way? Then this app can help. Women Who Changed the World uses beautiful illustrations to tell the stories of inspiring women like Marie Curie, Wangari Maathai, Rosa Parks, Frida Kahlo, Jane Goodall, Amelia Earhart, and Malala Yousafzai. The app is $3.99 but for this price you get higher-quality art styling and you also support all-female team that made this app.

Women Who Changed the World Developer: Learny Land
Price: 3.99
4.6
Download

Kids Doodle (All ages)

3 Images

Kids Doodle is a great drawing app for children. They can paint on a photo or a canvas, and there are 24 brushes to choose from. Additionally, the app comes with a movie mode that can play back the artwork like a small movie. As the app is designed for kids, it's extremely easy to use. Kids Doodle is free to download and use. There are no in-app purchases as well.

Kids Doodle - Paint & Draw Developer: Doodle Joy Studio
Price: Free
4.3
Download

Best Entertainment Apps for Kids

YouTube Kids (Ages up to 12)

3 Images

YouTube Kids, as its name suggests, is a version of YouTube that includes content specifically for children. It only features family-friendly content and includes a number of parental controls. Parents can limit screen time, check what their kids are watching, block videos, and flag content they deem inappropriate. YouTube Kids is free to download and use, but it includes ads.

YouTube Kids Developer: Google LLC
Price: Free
4.3
Download

Amazon Kids+ (Ages 3 to 12)

3 Images

For those who are already subscribers to Amazon Prime, the Amazon Kids+ is a great deal for all-in-one entertainment. For just $48, you get a year-long subscription to over 10,000 kids movies, TV shows, books and games. Some of this content includes major brands like Disney, Nickelodeon, PBS Kids, Marvel, Lego, while others is original. Your child will be sure to find something they love on Amazon Kids+. Note that the yearly subscription is slightly less worth it at $79 for non-Prime members.

Amazon Kids+: Books, Videosâ¦ Developer: Amazon Mobile LLC
Price: Free
3.9
Download

Nick Jr (Ages 3 to 8)

3 Images

The Nick Jr. app from Nickelodeon offers access to Nick Jr. episodes for free, and if you subscribe to the channel on TV, you can get access to even more episodes and content. The show selection includes Blue's Clues & You!, PAW Patrol, Bubble Guppies, Rubble & Crew, Baby Shark's Big Show, Peppa Pig, and Dora the Explorer.

Additionally, the app includes original videos, educational games, and more. The app is free to download and use. However, it does include ads.

Nick Jr - Watch Kids TV Shows Developer: Nickelodeon
Price: Free
3.7
Download

iHeartRadio Family

3 Images

The iHeartRadio Family app is one of the best music players for kids that you'll find. It offers a wide variety of music and radio stations that children will love, from classic tunes on Sesame Street to inspiring songs from Disney Princesses, all for free. It also comes with kid-friendly comedy, stories, podcasts, and more. Plus, it includes the ability to lock certain radio stations behind a PIN if you'd prefer your child doesn't listen to it.

iHeartRadio Family Developer: iHeartMedia, Inc.
Price: Free
4.1
Download

Best Android Games for Kids

Lego Friends: Heartlake Rush (Ages 4 to 12)

3 Images

Lego Friends: Heartlake Rush is a casual offline game where the player joins a cast of colorful Lego Friends characters to complete missions and races around Heartlake City. It offers a variety of mini-games and loads of customization, so it will have plenty of replay value for a child of the appropriate age. This game does have in-app purchases, but they are not necessary to fully enjoy the game.

LEGOÂ® Friends: Heartlake Rush Developer: StoryToys
Price: Free
4.2
Download

Monument Valley 2 (Ages 4 and up)

3 Images

Monument Valley 2 is an award-winning paid puzzle game that will be loved by your child. It includes beautifully crafted puzzles as a part of a story that's not only engaging but enjoyable as well. Moreover, the game looks amazing with its great visuals and melodic audio. The game is $3.99 but sometimes can be availed at discounts, often during festival season.

Monument Valley 2 Developer: ustwo games
Price: 3.99
4.7
Download

Toca Builders (Ages 5 and up)

3 Images

Toca Builders is a block-building game where children can build an entirely new world on an island. The game includes various characters that can do different tasks. As there are no rules or time limits, the game allows kids to let their creativity run wild and build what they want. It's also easy to use and has a kid-friendly interface. It's a paid game, and there are no in-app purchases.

Toca Builders Developer: Toca Boca
Price: 3.99
3.9
Download

Kahoot! Play & Create Quizzes (Ages 5 and up)

3 Images

If you're looking for a trivia game for your child, then this app from Kahoot! is great. It allows users to play, create, and even host quizzes. These quizzes will help your child learn new things. Moreover, it can be accessed in English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Brazilian Portuguese, and Norwegian.

Kahoot! Play & Create Quizzes is free to download and use, but there are optional in-app purchases for upgrades.

Kahoot! Play & Create Quizzes Developer: Kahoot!
Price: Free
4.7
Download

Pocket Build (Ages 10 and up)

3 Images

Pocket Build is a Minecraft-style sandbox game in which children can build their own fantasy world. There are hundreds of items that can be used to build things, with the open-world allowing endless possibilities. In the game’s survival and first-person modes, the users can also mine and collect resources. If you like Minecraft, you’ll love this game.

It's free to download, but there are in-app purchases. Also note that the game does feature some fantasy violence, hence the higher age category.

Pocket Build Developer: MOONBEAR
Price: Free
4.3
Download

Mario Kart Tour (Ages 8 and up)

3 Images

You'd be hard-pressed to find a kid who doesn't love racing around castles, shopping malls, desserts, and gardens as one of their favorite characters from the Super Mario franchise. Mario Kart Tour brings all the fun from traditional game consoles right to your Android phone or tablet. With more courses than you can count, tokens to collect, prizes to win, and streaks to set, Mario Kart Tour is a great game for all ages.

The best Android apps and games for kids: the bottom line

The Google Play Store is filled with thousands of apps and games for kids, but not all of them can be trusted equally. Some apps use predatory in-app purchase systems while others constantly disrupt your child's screen time with ads. That's why we tracked down the top Android apps for kids across a number of categories.

For example, Khan Academy Kids offers ad-free and micro transaction-free educational courses in a variety of topics. YouTube Kids provides video entertainment for all age groups with many different ways for parents to monitor what their kids watch. Lego Friends: Heartlake Rush offers fun Subway Surfers-like gameplay with tons of customization options. Any of these make a great starting point, but experiment with a couple of apps to find something your kid truly loves.

Meanwhile, if you're looking for more amazing Android apps, we've selected some of the best music player apps as well as the best wallpaper apps for Android.
