Pros
  • DDR5 RAM and PCIe 5.0 SSD support
  • Excellent connectivity
  • Decent power delivery
Cons
  • PCie 4.0 for GPU
  • Pricey given the specs
$220 at Amazon $220 at Newegg $240 at Best Buy

This may not be the most impressive motherboard using the B650 chipset from AMD, but one shouldn't overlook it for a capable gaming PC build. The Asus TUF Gaming B650-PLUS motherboard comes with plenty of features you need, and none of the fancy features you don't, making it a perfect choice for the budget-conscious AMD builder. There's full support for PCIe 5.0 SSDs, dual-GPU configurations, and four DIMM slots for up to 128GB of DDR5 RAM. You even get heatsink covers for the M.2 slots. The motherboard also comes with integrated Wi-Fi 6 and 2.5GbE networking with first-party TUF features, making this a well-rounded board for the price.

The 12+2 stage power design for delivering electricity isn't the best, but it'll easily handle even the flagship AMD Ryzen 7000 series processor. You could get away with some minor overclocking, but we wouldn't recommend pushing many components hard with the Asus TUF Gaming B650-PLUS. Where this motherboard shines is in its connectivity, price, and value. You'll be able to install a CPU, fire up the BIOS to make some adjustments, enabling AMD EXPO and setting fan curves, and be playing your favorite PC games in no time at all.

Source: ASUS
ASUS ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming WIFI
Best compact

For those creating a small form factor gaming PC

$428 $470 Save $42

This compact ITX ASUS ROG Strix X670E motherboard has everything you'll need for a powerful PC that can fit inside a smaller form factor chassis.

Chipset
AMD X670
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
10+2
PCI Slots
1x PCIe 5.0 x16
Storage
2x SATA, 1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 1x PCIe 4.0 M.2
Audio
ALC4050
Networking
Intel 2.5Gb, Wi-Fi 6E
Internal I/O
1x USB 3.2-C Gen 2, 1x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1
Rear I/O
2x USB-C 4.0, 5x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2, 3x USB-A 2.0
Pros
  • PCIe 5.0 and DDR5 support
  • Impressive features for the size
  • Excellent BIOS support
Cons
  • Expensive
  • Limited overclocking
$428 at Amazon $470 at Newegg

If you're looking to create a compact system, there's a strong possibility the PC case you'll be using requires a Mini-ITX motherboard. We've compiled a list of the best Mini-ITX motherboards and feel as though the Asus ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming Wi-Fi is the motherboard to buy. It's the smallest motherboard in our collection here, rocking just one PCI slot for the GPU and two DIMM slots for system memory. This restricts what one can do with the board, for instance only being able to install up to 64GB of DDR5 RAM, but it is possible to install the mighty AMD Ryzen 9 7950X and run the processor without an issue.

There's full support for Wi-Fi 6, though 2.5GbE networking is also present for those who have their PC near the router or switch. Plenty of USB ports are present on the rear, though you may need to invest in a fan splitter or two for connecting all the necessary case and CPU fans to the board. We'd recommend either one of the best air coolers or an AIO liquid cooler with the Asus ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming Wi-Fi. The 10+2 phase power delivery design ensures enough stable and reliable electricity will run to the CPU and other important components. You won't be able to overclock this thing, but it'll handle all the latest games and then some.

Source: NZXT
NZXT N7 B650E Gaming Wi-Fi 6 Motherboard
Best design

This NZXT motherboard is packed full of features

stylish metal-clad motherboard for clean white builds.

Brand
NZXT
Memory Slots
4
Memory Type
DDR5-6000+
Form Factor
ATX
CPU Support
Ryzen 7000+
Chipset
B650
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
16+2+1
PCI Slots
1x PCIe 5.0 x16, 2x PCIe 4.0 x16
Storage
4x SATA, 1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 2x PCIe 4.0 M.2
Audio
Realtek ALC1220
Networking
Realtek 2.5Gb, 2x2 Wi-Fi 6E (802.11 a/b/g/n/ac/ax)
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 3x USB-A 2.0
Rear I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 port, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, 4x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, 2x USB-A 2.0 ports, 1x HDMI 2.1, 5x Audio jacks, S/PDIF out
Pros
  • DDR5 RAM and PCIe 5.0 SSD support
  • Stunning design
  • Great set of features for a B650 board
Cons
  • Expensive for a B650 board
$317 at Amazon $317 at Newegg $320 at NZXT

The NZXT N7 B650E is the unique motherboard in this collection of AMD boards. The design makes use of large shrouds to cover most of the main PCB, resulting in a clean look. This also doubles as an easy way to personalize the motherboard with detailing on the removable shroud panels. NZXT tends to use mid-range chipsets and push them to their limits in terms of connectivity and performance and the N7 B650E is no exception.

The B650 chipset is great for those who don't require flagship features with AMD processors. The NZXT N7 B650E has three full-size x16 PCI slots with the primary slot supporting PCIe 5.0. There are three M.2 slots with one supporting PCIe 5.0 and four SATA ports at hand for connecting other storage drives. 2.5GbE networking is present alongside Wi-Fi 6E and you'll have access to more USB ports than you'll likely know what to do with.

Choosing the best AM5 motherboard for gaming

The Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming is a perfect example of a motherboard done right. It's expensive, but you'll have almost everything you need to build one of the best AMD-powered systems, including PCIe 5.0, DDR5 support, overclocking potential, a rock-solid BIOS, fast networking, and more. If you're on a tighter budget (or simply do not feel comfortable spending more than $400 on a motherboard), we'd recommend the excellent Asus TUF Gaming B650-Plus.

Source: ASUS
Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming
Best overall

The Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming is a high-end Asus motherboard that supports PCIe 5.0 graphics, three PCIe 5.0 SSDs, and high-end Ryzen 7000 CPUs thanks to its large 18+2 stage VRM.

$480 at Amazon $480 at Newegg $480 at B&H

item.title: Best docking stations for the Lenovo ThinkPad T14 Gen 3 in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best docking stations for the Lenovo ThinkPad T14 Gen 3 in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
The Lenovo ThinkPad T14 Gen 3 is now a year old, but it's still plenty powerful and modern. It packs in 12th-generation Intel Core vPro and AMD Ryzen 6000 PRO processors, a 14-inch 16:10 display, and more. Like many great ThinkPads, the connectivity is also pretty solid. It includes two Thunderbolt 4 ports on the Intel models, two USB Type-C (USB 3.2 Gen 2) ports on the AMD ones, an RJ45 Ethernet port, a SIM card slot, and more. If you're planning to use it at your desk, though, you'll probably want to consider a docking station to increase the number of ports. We did the digging, and found some of the best docking stations that are available to purchase right now:

item.title: Best Samsung Galaxy Buds Live cases in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best Samsung Galaxy Buds Live cases in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Galaxy Buds Live is one of the latest additions by Samsung, superseding Galaxy Buds 2 and Galaxy Buds 2 Pro. With the latest bean-type design, the quality has been significantly improved making them one of the best budget-centric wireless earbuds. However, protecting this delicate equipment requires the right cover for the case.

Since the external case of the Galaxy Buds Live is made up of plastic, it can accumulate a lot of scratches over time just like your smartphone. To protect the case from drops and surface damage, it's best to wrap it in a case. Below we have rounded up the best cases for the Galaxy Buds Live, with options ranging from simple and rugged cases to stylish ones.

item.title: watchOS 10: Smart Stack, new features, and everything you need to know
item.itunes:title: watchOS 10: Smart Stack, new features, and everything you need to know
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Quick Links

  • When will watchOS 10 be released?
  • What's new in watchOS 10?
  • Can my Apple Watch run watchOS 10?
  • How can I install watchOS 10?

There are only a few more weeks left to go until watchOS 10, the upcoming software update for the Apple Watch, starts rolling out to users worldwide. Although it isn't publicly available just yet, Apple gave us a preview of what's to come with watchOS 10 at WWDC in June 2023 and in betas. watchOS 10 looks to be one of the biggest software updates to the Apple Watch in years, marked by new buttons and gestures. They're designed to implement Smart Stack, which is a new way to view information at a glance on your smartwatch. Here's everything you need to know about watchOS 10, including new features, release dates, and compatible devices.

When will watchOS 10 be released?

Apple announced watchOS 10 alongside iOS 17, iPadOS 17, tvOS 17, and macOS Sonoma at WWDC on June 5, 2023. Developer betas of watchOS 10 are available now, and Apple opened up the public beta program in July. The full watchOS 10 release is slated for this fall, and it usually comes in late September.

What's new in watchOS 10?

The biggest change to watchOS 10 is Smart Stack, which gives Apple Watch users a new way to interact with their smartwatch. But there are a ton of other feature additions, too, especially when it comes to health and fitness. Here's a list of all the key changes:

Smart Stack is a new way to navigate your watch

Complications have been on the Apple Watch for some time, giving users the ability to see key information at a glance. That's taken to the next level with Smart Stack in watchOS 10. Similar to a feature by the same name in iOS, a Smart Stack is a group of widgets that appears on your watch face and cycles automatically. For example, the Weather widget might show you the daily forecast in the morning, or the Wallet widget might show your boarding pass when you're traveling.

Like Smart Stack on your iPhone, you can adjust the order of the widgets in watchOS 10. You can turn the Digital Crown to cycle through it in real time. Since this feature can essentially replace complications, it works best on full-screen watch faces, like the Photo watch face.

Finally, a full-screen app experience

Apple keeps making the Apple Watch display bigger, but watchOS hasn't made the best use of the newfound space. But a slew of apps have been redesigned in watchOS 10 to make the most of big-screen Apple Watches, including the Music app. Apple confirmed that the Weather, Stocks, Home, Maps, Messages, and World Clock apps will have a new full-screen experience as well, with more to come.

New watch faces coming to watchOS 10

Source: Apple

With every watchOS update comes new watch faces, and watchOS 10 will get a few new ones. There's a Pallette face that shifts between colors as the time changes, along with a Peanuts watch face that features Snoopy and Woodstock. Unfortunately, there is still no official way to get third-party watch faces on the Apple Watch, and that isn't changing with watchOS 10.

Workout Views on Apple Watch and iPhone

2 Images

Apple brought Workout Views to the Apple Watch last year, and they're now coming to the iPhone. When you start a workout on your Apple Watch in watchOS 10, it can appear on your iPhone as a Live Activity. When tapped, that Live Activity becomes full screen, and it shows your Workout Views. This is great for activities like cycling, where you can mount your iPhone to a handlebar instead of trying to glance at your Apple Watch.

Waypoints get better on watchOS 10, and it could save your life

Source: Apple

Apple introduced Waypoints with watchOS 9, and your Apple Watch will automatically record two new ones with watchOS 10: a Last Cellular Connection Waypoint and a Last Emergency Call Waypoint. The Last Cellular Connection Waypoint records the last location where your Apple Watch was able to connect to cellular data, and the waypoint can help you get back to that spot. Similarly, the Last Emergency Call Waypoint records the last point where your Apple Watch was able to connect to any cellular network. By recording this point, your Apple Watch can help you find a location where you can make an emergency call.

Other features coming to watchOS 10

Aside from the above features, there are other new changes coming to watchOS 10, and here is the full list:

  • You will be able to access the Control Center by pressing the side button.
  • Apple Watches can connect to more Bluetooth-enabled health accessories.
  • Apple Maps can now display a topographical map in the United States.
  • The Mindfulness app lets users log their thoughts and states of mind on their wrist.
  • Apple Watches can use their ambient light sensor to track the amount of time spent outdoors.
  • NameDrop, a contact-sharing feature coming to iOS 17, is also coming to Apple Watch.
  • Group FaceTime audio is coming to the Apple Watch.

Can my Apple Watch run watchOS 10?

Though Apple is known for its robust software support, not all Apple Watches can upgrade to watchOS 10. Upgrading to watchOS 10 requires an Apple Watch to be paired to an iPhone XS, XR, or newer that is running iOS 17. The watch itself must be an Apple Watch Series 4, Apple Watch SE, or newer. We also expect the rumored Apple Watch Series 9 and Apple Watch Ultra 2 to ship with watchOS 10 pre-installed. Here's the full list of compatible models:

  • Apple Watch Series 4
  • Apple Watch Series 5
  • Apple Watch SE
  • Apple Watch Series 6
  • Apple Watch Series 7
  • Apple Watch Series 8
  • Apple Watch Ultra

Luckily, all the Apple Watches that support watchOS 9 can also run watchOS 10. However, it should be noted that not every watchOS 10 feature will be available on each device. Some are exclusive to newer models, like the Apple Watch Series 8 and Apple Watch Ultra.

How can I install watchOS 10?

Since watchOS 10 won't be publicly available until the fall, only users registered in the Apple Developer Program or the Apple Beta Software Program can install the update now. People interested in running the developer beta can follow our guide to installing the watchOS 10 beta. There's also a more stable public beta available through the Apple Beta Software Program. We expect the full watchOS 10 release to debut sometime before the end of September following Apple's fall event.
item.description:
Quick Links

  • When will watchOS 10 be released?
  • What's new in watchOS 10?
  • Can my Apple Watch run watchOS 10?
  • How can I install watchOS 10?

There are only a few more weeks left to go until watchOS 10, the upcoming software update for the Apple Watch, starts rolling out to users worldwide. Although it isn't publicly available just yet, Apple gave us a preview of what's to come with watchOS 10 at WWDC in June 2023 and in betas. watchOS 10 looks to be one of the biggest software updates to the Apple Watch in years, marked by new buttons and gestures. They're designed to implement Smart Stack, which is a new way to view information at a glance on your smartwatch. Here's everything you need to know about watchOS 10, including new features, release dates, and compatible devices.

When will watchOS 10 be released?

Apple announced watchOS 10 alongside iOS 17, iPadOS 17, tvOS 17, and macOS Sonoma at WWDC on June 5, 2023. Developer betas of watchOS 10 are available now, and Apple opened up the public beta program in July. The full watchOS 10 release is slated for this fall, and it usually comes in late September.

What's new in watchOS 10?

The biggest change to watchOS 10 is Smart Stack, which gives Apple Watch users a new way to interact with their smartwatch. But there are a ton of other feature additions, too, especially when it comes to health and fitness. Here's a list of all the key changes:

Smart Stack is a new way to navigate your watch

Complications have been on the Apple Watch for some time, giving users the ability to see key information at a glance. That's taken to the next level with Smart Stack in watchOS 10. Similar to a feature by the same name in iOS, a Smart Stack is a group of widgets that appears on your watch face and cycles automatically. For example, the Weather widget might show you the daily forecast in the morning, or the Wallet widget might show your boarding pass when you're traveling.

Like Smart Stack on your iPhone, you can adjust the order of the widgets in watchOS 10. You can turn the Digital Crown to cycle through it in real time. Since this feature can essentially replace complications, it works best on full-screen watch faces, like the Photo watch face.

Finally, a full-screen app experience

Apple keeps making the Apple Watch display bigger, but watchOS hasn't made the best use of the newfound space. But a slew of apps have been redesigned in watchOS 10 to make the most of big-screen Apple Watches, including the Music app. Apple confirmed that the Weather, Stocks, Home, Maps, Messages, and World Clock apps will have a new full-screen experience as well, with more to come.

New watch faces coming to watchOS 10

Source: Apple

With every watchOS update comes new watch faces, and watchOS 10 will get a few new ones. There's a Pallette face that shifts between colors as the time changes, along with a Peanuts watch face that features Snoopy and Woodstock. Unfortunately, there is still no official way to get third-party watch faces on the Apple Watch, and that isn't changing with watchOS 10.

Workout Views on Apple Watch and iPhone

2 Images

Apple brought Workout Views to the Apple Watch last year, and they're now coming to the iPhone. When you start a workout on your Apple Watch in watchOS 10, it can appear on your iPhone as a Live Activity. When tapped, that Live Activity becomes full screen, and it shows your Workout Views. This is great for activities like cycling, where you can mount your iPhone to a handlebar instead of trying to glance at your Apple Watch.

Waypoints get better on watchOS 10, and it could save your life

Source: Apple

Apple introduced Waypoints with watchOS 9, and your Apple Watch will automatically record two new ones with watchOS 10: a Last Cellular Connection Waypoint and a Last Emergency Call Waypoint. The Last Cellular Connection Waypoint records the last location where your Apple Watch was able to connect to cellular data, and the waypoint can help you get back to that spot. Similarly, the Last Emergency Call Waypoint records the last point where your Apple Watch was able to connect to any cellular network. By recording this point, your Apple Watch can help you find a location where you can make an emergency call.

Other features coming to watchOS 10

Aside from the above features, there are other new changes coming to watchOS 10, and here is the full list:

  • You will be able to access the Control Center by pressing the side button.
  • Apple Watches can connect to more Bluetooth-enabled health accessories.
  • Apple Maps can now display a topographical map in the United States.
  • The Mindfulness app lets users log their thoughts and states of mind on their wrist.
  • Apple Watches can use their ambient light sensor to track the amount of time spent outdoors.
  • NameDrop, a contact-sharing feature coming to iOS 17, is also coming to Apple Watch.
  • Group FaceTime audio is coming to the Apple Watch.

Can my Apple Watch run watchOS 10?

Though Apple is known for its robust software support, not all Apple Watches can upgrade to watchOS 10. Upgrading to watchOS 10 requires an Apple Watch to be paired to an iPhone XS, XR, or newer that is running iOS 17. The watch itself must be an Apple Watch Series 4, Apple Watch SE, or newer. We also expect the rumored Apple Watch Series 9 and Apple Watch Ultra 2 to ship with watchOS 10 pre-installed. Here's the full list of compatible models:

  • Apple Watch Series 4
  • Apple Watch Series 5
  • Apple Watch SE
  • Apple Watch Series 6
  • Apple Watch Series 7
  • Apple Watch Series 8
  • Apple Watch Ultra

Luckily, all the Apple Watches that support watchOS 9 can also run watchOS 10. However, it should be noted that not every watchOS 10 feature will be available on each device. Some are exclusive to newer models, like the Apple Watch Series 8 and Apple Watch Ultra.

How can I install watchOS 10?

Since watchOS 10 won't be publicly available until the fall, only users registered in the Apple Developer Program or the Apple Beta Software Program can install the update now. People interested in running the developer beta can follow our guide to installing the watchOS 10 beta. There's also a more stable public beta available through the Apple Beta Software Program. We expect the full watchOS 10 release to debut sometime before the end of September following Apple's fall event.

  • You will be able to access the Control Center by pressing the side button.
  • Apple Watches can connect to more Bluetooth-enabled health accessories.
  • Apple Maps can now display a topographical map in the United States.
  • The Mindfulness app lets users log their thoughts and states of mind on their wrist.
  • Apple Watches can use their ambient light sensor to track the amount of time spent outdoors.
  • NameDrop, a contact-sharing feature coming to iOS 17, is also coming to Apple Watch.
  • Group FaceTime audio is coming to the Apple Watch.

Can my Apple Watch run watchOS 10?

Though Apple is known for its robust software support, not all Apple Watches can upgrade to watchOS 10. Upgrading to watchOS 10 requires an Apple Watch to be paired to an iPhone XS, XR, or newer that is running iOS 17. The watch itself must be an Apple Watch Series 4, Apple Watch SE, or newer. We also expect the rumored Apple Watch Series 9 and Apple Watch Ultra 2 to ship with watchOS 10 pre-installed. Here's the full list of compatible models:

  • Apple Watch Series 4
  • Apple Watch Series 5
  • Apple Watch SE
  • Apple Watch Series 6
  • Apple Watch Series 7
  • Apple Watch Series 8
  • Apple Watch Ultra

Luckily, all the Apple Watches that support watchOS 9 can also run watchOS 10. However, it should be noted that not every watchOS 10 feature will be available on each device. Some are exclusive to newer models, like the Apple Watch Series 8 and Apple Watch Ultra.

How can I install watchOS 10?

Since watchOS 10 won't be publicly available until the fall, only users registered in the Apple Developer Program or the Apple Beta Software Program can install the update now. People interested in running the developer beta can follow our guide to installing the watchOS 10 beta. There's also a more stable public beta available through the Apple Beta Software Program. We expect the full watchOS 10 release to debut sometime before the end of September following Apple's fall event.

item.title: Best docking stations for LG Gram Style (2023)
item.itunes:title: Best docking stations for LG Gram Style (2023)
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
The LG Gram Style is one of the most beautiful laptops on the market today. If you've used it, you've most likely been transfixed by the gorgeous chassis, stunning OLED display, and unique touchpad. It is so lightweight that it's barely over two pounds. It became available back in March and there are two versions of it: a 14-inch model and a 16-inch one. There's a lot you can do with this, but adding more ports will make any of the best laptops that much better. If you're looking for the best docking station to get more out of your LG Gram Style while you're at home, we're here to help. See our picks below.

item.title: Best motherboards for AMD CPUs in 2023
item.itunes:title: Best motherboards for AMD CPUs in 2023
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Choosing the best motherboard for AMD processors usually involves picking one with an AM5 socket and chipset for the latest Ryzen chips. Depending on what you plan on using the system for, it's possible to spend anywhere between $100 and $1,000 on a motherboard for the best CPUs from AMD. This collection includes our favorite motherboard recommendations for AMD processors.

  • Source: ASUS
    Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming
    Best overall
    $480 at Amazon
  • GIGABYTE X670 AORUS Elite AX
    Runner-up
    $260 at Amazon
  • Source: ASUS
    Asus TUF Gaming B650-Plus
    Best value AM5
    $220 at Amazon
  • Source: ASUS
    ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi
    Best value AM4
    $181 at Amazon
  • Source: NZXT
    NZXT N7 B650E Gaming Wi-Fi 6 Motherboard
    Best design
    $317 at Amazon
  • Source: ASUS
    ASUS ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming WIFI
    Best compact
    $428 at Amazon
  • Source: MSI
    MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE Motherboard
    Best for enthusiasts
    $1262 at Amazon
  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock WRX80 Creator
    Best workstation
    $900 at Newegg

Our top picks for the best AMD motherboards in 2023

Source: ASUS
Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming
Best overall

The best AMD motherboard you can buy

The Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming is a high-end Asus motherboard that supports PCIe 5.0 graphics, three PCIe 5.0 SSDs, and high-end Ryzen 7000 CPUs thanks to its large 18+2 stage VRM.

Memory Type
DDR5-6400+
Form Factor
ATX
Chipset
X670
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
18+2
PCI Slots
2x PCIe 5.0 x16, 1x PCie 4.0 x16
Storage
4x SATA, 2x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 2x PCIe 4.0 M.2
Audio
Realtek ALC4080
Networking
Intel 2.5Gb, 2x2 Wi-Fi 6E (802.11 a/b/g/n/ac/ax)
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2, 1x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 3x USB-A 2.0, 1x Thunderbolt header
Rear I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 port, 10x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, 2x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 ports
Pros
  • Excellent performance
  • Five M.2 SSD slots
  • Good overclocking support
Cons
  • Pricey
  • No 5Gb LAN
$480 at Amazon $480 at Newegg $480 at B&H

One of our favorite motherboards for AMD Ryzen 7000 processors is the ASUS ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming. It has almost everything you need for a stable PC build, even with the flagship AMD Ryzen 9 7950X. Let's start with the X670E chipset. It's an "extreme" version of the X670, reserved for more enthusiast-grade boards due to it being better for overclocking and having all the connectivity in the world. We've got an 18+2 phase power delivery design for the CPU and other nearby components, as well as two PCIe 5.0 x16 slots, one PCIe 4.0 x16 slot, three PCIe 5.0 M.2 SSD slots, and a single PCIe 4.0 M.2 SSD slot.

Source: XDA-Developers

This motherboard can work with the latest GPUs and storage, as well as many future products down the line. We're not using anywhere close to the limit of PCIe 5.0 for graphics cards, making the ASUS ROG Strix X670-E Gaming a good choice for the future. For USB ports on the rear I/O, ASUS made sure you have plenty of options, including 12 USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, one USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 port, and a further three more USB 2.0 ports. This is all rounded out with 2.5Gb networking for speedy access to your LAN and the outside world. It's simply one of the better X670E motherboards without spending a ridiculous amount of money.

GIGABYTE X670 AORUS Elite AX
Runner-up

Another great AM5 motherboard choice

$260 $280 Save $20

The Gigabyte X670 Aorus Elite AX has the essentials for a high-end gaming PC, including support for DDR5-6666 memory, four M.2 slots for SSDs (one of which supports PCIe 5.0), and a 16-stage VRM.

Memory Type
DDR5-6666+
Form Factor
ATX
Chipset
AMD X670
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
16+2+2
PCI Slots
1x PCIe 4.0 x16, 1x PCIe 4.0 x4, 1x PCIe 3.0 x1
Storage
1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 2x PCIe 4.0 M.2, 4x SATA
Networking
2.5 GbE, AMD Wi-Fi 6E RZ616
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB 2.0
Rear I/O
6x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2, 1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2, 4x USB-A 2.0
Pros
  • Great performance
  • Good overclock potential
  • Connectivity
Cons
  • No PCIe 5.0 slot
$260 at Amazon $280 at Newegg $280 at Best Buy

The Gigabyte X670 AORUS Elite AX is another top recommendation from us for AMD Ryzen 7000+ processors and the AM5 socket. Just like the Asus X670 motherboard we recommended above, this AORUS Elite AX board has plenty of premium features, including speedy networking, rapid M.2 storage slots, and decent power delivery for some overclocking. We're looking at the same standard ATX form factor, which means this motherboard will fit inside most PC cases. Beefy heatsinks cover the VRMs and with a 16+2+2 phase power delivery design, you shouldn't encounter any issues regarding heat.

As you'd expect from a premium motherboard, the M.2 slots are also covered with heatsinks/shroud, making it a great overall environment for cooling. Additionally, this motherboard sports a USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 port for the front panel. The rear I/O has numerous connection points, including 2.5GbE networking, six USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, two USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, one USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 port, and four USB-A 2.0 ports. You won't have any trouble hooking up all your gaming accessories to a system built with this motherboard. It also looks great and should fit in with most PC build themes.

The Gigabyte X670 AORUS Elite AX motherboard has two 8-pin connectors to feed power to the CPU. It goes without saying that you also get a ton of fan/pump, USB, and RGB headers on this motherboard. These headers are usually available in plenty of motherboards now, so it's nothing unusual. You also get up to four DRAM slots capable of running up to 128GB of DDR4 RAM with up to DDR5-6666+ speeds. Your mileage is bound to vary regarding speeds since not all memory modules have the same speeds. That being said, it's good to see the support for some of the fastest RAM kits available on the market.

The motherboard also comes with three PCIe slots. Two of those are full-length reinforced slots, whereas the last one is an x1 slot at the bottom without any reinforcement. We also like the fact that the I/O shield is pre-installed on this motherboard, which means that's one less step to worry about while building a PC. Overall, the Gigabyte X670 AORUS Elite AX is a fantastic motherboard that's capable of powering even the most demanding CPUs on the market. Not to mention, it also supports overclocking, so enthusiasts would love to have this board at their disposal for a fully decked-out AMD-based build.

Source: ASUS
Asus TUF Gaming B650-Plus
Best value AM5

Save some money and still build a powerful PC

The Asus TUF Gaming B650-Plus is a midrange motherboard with support for PCIe 5.0 SSDs and midrange to high-end Ryzen 7000 CPUs.

Form Factor
ATX
Chipset
AMD B650
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
12+2
PCI Slots
2x PCIe 4.0 x16, 2x PCIe 4.0 x1
Storage
4x SATA, 1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 2x PCIe 4.0 M.2
Networking
1x Realtek 2.5Gb
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 1, 1x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB-A 2.0
Rear I/O
2x USB 3.2 Gen 2, 4x USB 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB 2.0
Pros
  • Great value
  • DDR5 and PCIe 5.0 support
Cons
  • Only three PCIe M.2 slots
  • Limited rear I/O
$220 at Amazon $220 at Newegg $240 at Best Buy

Going with the latest AMD Ryzen processors doesn't mean you need to spend a fortune, even on a motherboard with an AM5 socket. The Asus TUF Gaming B650-PLUS rocks the AMD B650 chipset, which only loses a few features compared to the flagship X670 chipset, and we've seen motherboard vendors work some serious magic with this thing. We've got a 12+2 phase power delivery design, which should allow you to perform a little overclocking, as well as a three-year limited warranty should anything happen to go wrong. The full-size ATX form factor allowed Asus to cram as many PCIe slots as possible and this board has two PCIe 4.0 x16 slots, followed by two additional PCIe 4.0 x1 slots.

For storage, you'll be able to utilize the array of SATA ports and two PCIe M.2 slots (one being PCIe 5.0, the other 4.0). Plenty of USB ports can be found on the rear, as well as the 2.5Gb networking, which is great for when upgrading your LAN further down the line. Four DIMM slots support a maximum of 128GB of DDR5 RAM, which is capable of hitting speeds up to 6400 MT/s. Even though we're looking at a lower-tier chipset and no PCIe 5.0 slots for GPUs and other expansion cards, Asus made sure to spend the necessary budget on some of the essentials.

The standard ATX form factor allowed Asus to space everything out for a cleaner aesthetic. There isn't a massive shroud covering most of the PCB and with everything being black, it's a stealthy-looking motherboard. It's not the most striking board in terms of design, but we've come to appreciate ASUS' more industrial look of its TUF products. If you're looking for one of the best-value AM5 motherboards for an AMD Ryzen 7000 PC build, consider giving the Asus TUF Gaming B650-PLUS a go.

Source: ASUS
ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi
Best value AM4

Keep your old Ryzen CPU alive with this budget board

The Asus ROG Strix B550-F Gaming is one of the better B550 boards on the market, and it's a great option for those looking to build a powerful AMD-based build with one of the 5000-series processors.

Form Factor
ATX
Chipset
AMD B550
Socket
AM4
Power Phases
12+2
PCI Slots

$181 at Amazon $190 at Newegg $181 at B&H

The ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi is one of the best B550 motherboards you can buy for your AMD CPUs. It offers most of the features and capabilities of more expensive X570-chipset-based boards with an affordable price tag. It may not be the best motherboard for your AMD CPU, but it is what offers the most value for your money and is perfect for a budget build. The ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi features a 14-phase delivery, 2.5GB LAN, integrated WiFi 6, two M.2 slots, and more. The motherboard VRM is covered with a heatsink, and it comes with two EPS connectors.

You also get a ton of fan/pump headers, in addition to a bunch of RGB and USB headers on the motherboard. DDR4 support is present for up to 5100 for system memory, allowing you to match this board with a high-power AMD Ryzen 5000-series processor. Two full-sized PCIe slots are available with the primary one being reinforced for accepting GPUs. It provides 16 PCIe 4.0 lanes to the GPU, whereas the second slot runs at PCIe 3.0 x4 speeds. In terms of connectivity, you get a total of eight USB ports which include two USB 3.2 Gen 2, four USB 3.1 Gen1, and two USB 2.0. One of the USB 2.0 ports is designated for BIOS flash functionality.

In addition to the USB ports, you also get a single Intel 2.5 GbE LAN port, an HDMI, and DisplayPort, along with an audio stack consisting of 5-plug analog outputs plus SPDIF. The performance of the ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi is also on-point. It supports even the most demanding AMD CPU on the market. That being said, it's best to pair this particular motherboard with, say, an AMD Ryzen 5 5600X CPU for a mid-range build instead of a high-end rig. As such, it shouldn't have any issues running any 3000 or 5000 series CPU thanks to the AM4 socket and the B550 chipset. This motherboard is readily available on the market right now, but be sure to check out this link below to find the best price online.

Source: NZXT

Pros

$317 at Amazon $317 at Newegg $320 at NZXT

NZXT made a splash in the motherboard space with its N7 Z490 board for Intel builds, following which it quickly became a popular choice for a lot of people. The company quickly followed up with an AMD version and now we've got a board built for AMD's AM5 platform. The NZXT N7 B650E is our pick for the best motherboard design. It's stunning with a matte black or white shroud. The N7 B650E comes with PCIe 5.0 support, Wi-Fi 6E, four SATA ports, and a ton of USB ports for connecting all kinds of peripherals. It's one of the best-looking motherboards on this list, which looks best when paired with an NZXT PC case.

The NZXT N7 B650E motherboard comes in a black-and-white color scheme. It comes with a rather sophisticated heatsink/shroud covering almost the entirety of the motherboard. The only visible part of the PCB is next to the AM5 CPU socket, which will eventually get covered by the CPU heatsink. The heavily-shrouded design isn't for everyone, but it'll nicely blend into any and all kinds of PC builds without any issues. Those who don't like RGB lights on their motherboard will particularly like the N7 B550 since it doesn't have any RGB lights whatsoever. So if you're into RGB lighting, you'll have to look elsewhere.

The NZXT N7 B650E comes with two EPS connectors and a bunch of headers for your fan/pump, RGB, and USB ports. You also get two 4-pin NZXT RGB headers to control compatible NZXT products. You also get four DRAM slots on this board, although none of them are reinforced. It supports up to 128GB of RAM with listed speeds of up to DDR5-6000 (OC). The board has three PCIe slots in total out of which only the top slot is reinforced for the GPU. You also get three M.2 slots that accept drives up to 80mm long. NZXT attempted to get the most out of the B650 chipset, rivaling feature sets of more expensive boards.

Just like other premium motherboards in this collection, the NZXT N7 B650E also comes with a pre-installed I/O shield. You get ten USB ports in total out of which four are USB 3.2 Gen2 ports, four are USB 3.2 Gen1 ports, and the final two are USB 2.0 ports. You also get an HDMI port along with BIOS flashback and clear CMOS buttons and a Wi-Fi antenna. The performance of the N7 B650E is on-par with a lot of other premium motherboards on the market. It's a fantastic option for those looking at a clean motherboard that hides the PCB design. It's particularly good for builds involving NZXT PC cases or other NZXT components.

Source: ASUS
ASUS ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming WIFI

Build a super-compact gaming PC with this board

$428 at Amazon $470 at Newegg

This ASUS ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming WiFi motherboard is one to buy if you fancy building a small form factor PC. It's seriously small, rocking just one PCI slot. This size does mean you will lose some features, including only having one PCI slot, but you're able to create something special with a smaller footprint. ASUS managed to include a 10+2 phase power delivery design and the flagship X670E chipset. This means you should be able to run even the AMD Ryzen 9 without issues, though overclocking may produce some unstable results with the higher-tier processors. The single PCIe slot we mentioned earlier is 5.0, so too is one of two available PCIe M.2 slots for SSDs.

There's not a lot to the board itself, due to the size, but ASUS did a fine job in making it aesthetically pleasing and stealthy enough to fit inside more themed builds. There are a few headers for cooling and RGB lighting integration, as well as all the usual power connections. An added bonus of the ASUS ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming WiFi is the inclusion of the ROG Strix Hive. This external device hooks up to the motherboard and offers additional functionality, including an integrated DAC. This can prove useful if you position your compact PC somewhere that makes the rear I/O difficult to access.

For rear I/O, Asus managed to squeeze in two USB-C 4.0 ports, five USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, and a further three USB-A 2.0 ports, so even the most compact PC system will be able to handle a few gaming peripherals.

Source: MSI
MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE Motherboard
Best for enthusiasts

Try your luck at the overclocking leaderboard!

The MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE is an extreme motherboard with just about everything you could ask for in an enthusiastic platform. Designed for heavier use, MSI offers multiple reinforced PCIe 5.0 slots, four M.2 slots, robust power delivery with effective VRM cooling, and a striking dark board design.

$1262 at Amazon $1300 at Newegg

You likely won't require anything close to the MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE but it's designed for those with more advanced hardware and plans for some overclocking. Let's start with the impressive power setup, consisting of a 24+2+1 phase delivery design. The VRMs are covered by a large heatsink and MSI made good use of some thermal pads and MOSFET passive cooling. Audio is handled by the Realtek ALC4082 codec with help from the ESS ES9280AQ, which means movies, music, and (most importantly) games will sound amazing when paired with the best gaming headset.

For RAM, MSI states the board will happily support DDR5 modules with speeds up to 5600MT/s. The DIMM slots are conveniently located next to the migrated 4-pin EPS sockets. In fact, just about every major connection header on this motherboard has been adjusted to face perpendicular to the motherboard, instead of outward. This should aid in cable routing, especially for the thicker cabling. All three PCI slots are reinforced and support PCIe 5.0 for maximum bandwidth. If all that wasn't enough, there are four M.2 slots (one being PCIe 5.0), and 10GbE networking.

The rear I/O is just as ridiculous with seven USB-A 3.2 Gen2 ports, and two USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 ports. The MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE is incredibly expensive, almost triple the price of some of our recommendations here, but the price may be worth it if you require all these advanced features not typically found on lesser boards.

Source: ASRock
ASRock WRX80 Creator
Best workstation

For serious computing and performance

The ASRock WRX80 is a monster of a motherboard designed specifically for AMD Ryzen Threadripper 3000 and 5000 series processors. Looking to build a powerful workstation or server? Start your build right here.

  • Incredible bandwidth

$900 at Newegg $900 at Amazon

Last but not least, we've added the ASRock WRX80 Creator as our pick for the best motherboard for those looking to build a HEDT PC with an AMD Ryzen Threadripper processor. The ASRock WRX80 Creator is one of the most expensive motherboards you can buy, but that's because it comes with a long list of features that are not necessarily found on any of the mainstream motherboards we've listed above. It serves up to 64 lanes of PCIe at Gen4 speeds. It's also only the motherboard in this list that comes with a dual-fan heatsink covering its 8 105A voltage regulator phases.

The ASRock WRX80 Creator is an ATX motherboard that carries an AMD sWRX socket. There are two 40mm fans cooling the VRM, in addition to the regular 50mm fan that we usually see on other mainstream AMD motherboards. These VRM fans are enough to tame even the most powerful, fully-overclocked system without a hitch. There are two available M.2 PCIe 4.0 slots for SSDs, joining the seven PCIe 4.0 x16 slots for expansion cards.

The I/O shield comes pre-installed, which isn't all that surprising considering this is a high-end premium motherboard. You won't see any RGB lighting, however. The ASRock WRX80 Creator comes with 8 DIMM slots for impressive system memory capacity. It also goes without saying that you get plenty of headers for fan/pump, RGB lights, USB, and more. Overclocking is also in cards since this is a high-performance motherboard that's designed to push even the most demanding HEDT CPUs on the market.

Choosing the best AMD motherboard

It's not required to spend a fortune to enjoy the best of what's offered by AMD desktop CPUs, and we believe our collection here provides ample choice, covering multiple generations. We view the ASUS ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming as the best X670 chipset-based motherboard one can buy right now for a high-end PC build. It has a solid power phase delivery system for stable CPU overclocking, plenty of connectivity, and an excellent set of features ... for a price.

The ASUS TUF Gaming B650-PLUS is also a great option for those looking to buy an affordable AM5 motherboard. It has many of the same features offered by the X670E-E Gaming from ASUS but cuts a few to bring the price down. If you're not needing the absolute best platform for enthusiast overclocking and likely won't saturate all the available PCIe lanes offered by the B650 chipset, this motherboard will be more than enough.

Source: ASUS
Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming
Best overall

The Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming is a high-end Asus motherboard that supports PCIe 5.0 graphics, three PCIe 5.0 SSDs, and high-end Ryzen 7000 CPUs thanks to its large 18+2 stage VRM.

$480 at Amazon $480 at Newegg $480 at B&H
item.description:
Choosing the best motherboard for AMD processors usually involves picking one with an AM5 socket and chipset for the latest Ryzen chips. Depending on what you plan on using the system for, it's possible to spend anywhere between $100 and $1,000 on a motherboard for the best CPUs from AMD. This collection includes our favorite motherboard recommendations for AMD processors.

The best AMD motherboard you can buy

The Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming is a high-end Asus motherboard that supports PCIe 5.0 graphics, three PCIe 5.0 SSDs, and high-end Ryzen 7000 CPUs thanks to its large 18+2 stage VRM.

$480 at Amazon $480 at Newegg $480 at B&H

One of our favorite motherboards for AMD Ryzen 7000 processors is the ASUS ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming. It has almost everything you need for a stable PC build, even with the flagship AMD Ryzen 9 7950X. Let's start with the X670E chipset. It's an "extreme" version of the X670, reserved for more enthusiast-grade boards due to it being better for overclocking and having all the connectivity in the world. We've got an 18+2 phase power delivery design for the CPU and other nearby components, as well as two PCIe 5.0 x16 slots, one PCIe 4.0 x16 slot, three PCIe 5.0 M.2 SSD slots, and a single PCIe 4.0 M.2 SSD slot.

Source: XDA-Developers

This motherboard can work with the latest GPUs and storage, as well as many future products down the line. We're not using anywhere close to the limit of PCIe 5.0 for graphics cards, making the ASUS ROG Strix X670-E Gaming a good choice for the future. For USB ports on the rear I/O, ASUS made sure you have plenty of options, including 12 USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, one USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 port, and a further three more USB 2.0 ports. This is all rounded out with 2.5Gb networking for speedy access to your LAN and the outside world. It's simply one of the better X670E motherboards without spending a ridiculous amount of money.

GIGABYTE X670 AORUS Elite AX
Runner-up

Another great AM5 motherboard choice

The Gigabyte X670 Aorus Elite AX has the essentials for a high-end gaming PC, including support for DDR5-6666 memory, four M.2 slots for SSDs (one of which supports PCIe 5.0), and a 16-stage VRM.

$260 at Amazon $280 at Newegg $280 at Best Buy

The Gigabyte X670 AORUS Elite AX is another top recommendation from us for AMD Ryzen 7000+ processors and the AM5 socket. Just like the Asus X670 motherboard we recommended above, this AORUS Elite AX board has plenty of premium features, including speedy networking, rapid M.2 storage slots, and decent power delivery for some overclocking. We're looking at the same standard ATX form factor, which means this motherboard will fit inside most PC cases. Beefy heatsinks cover the VRMs and with a 16+2+2 phase power delivery design, you shouldn't encounter any issues regarding heat.

As you'd expect from a premium motherboard, the M.2 slots are also covered with heatsinks/shroud, making it a great overall environment for cooling. Additionally, this motherboard sports a USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 port for the front panel. The rear I/O has numerous connection points, including 2.5GbE networking, six USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, two USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, one USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 port, and four USB-A 2.0 ports. You won't have any trouble hooking up all your gaming accessories to a system built with this motherboard. It also looks great and should fit in with most PC build themes.

The Gigabyte X670 AORUS Elite AX motherboard has two 8-pin connectors to feed power to the CPU. It goes without saying that you also get a ton of fan/pump, USB, and RGB headers on this motherboard. These headers are usually available in plenty of motherboards now, so it's nothing unusual. You also get up to four DRAM slots capable of running up to 128GB of DDR4 RAM with up to DDR5-6666+ speeds. Your mileage is bound to vary regarding speeds since not all memory modules have the same speeds. That being said, it's good to see the support for some of the fastest RAM kits available on the market.

The motherboard also comes with three PCIe slots. Two of those are full-length reinforced slots, whereas the last one is an x1 slot at the bottom without any reinforcement. We also like the fact that the I/O shield is pre-installed on this motherboard, which means that's one less step to worry about while building a PC. Overall, the Gigabyte X670 AORUS Elite AX is a fantastic motherboard that's capable of powering even the most demanding CPUs on the market. Not to mention, it also supports overclocking, so enthusiasts would love to have this board at their disposal for a fully decked-out AMD-based build.

Source: ASUS
Asus TUF Gaming B650-Plus
Best value AM5

Save some money and still build a powerful PC

$220 at Amazon $220 at Newegg $240 at Best Buy

Going with the latest AMD Ryzen processors doesn't mean you need to spend a fortune, even on a motherboard with an AM5 socket. The Asus TUF Gaming B650-PLUS rocks the AMD B650 chipset, which only loses a few features compared to the flagship X670 chipset, and we've seen motherboard vendors work some serious magic with this thing. We've got a 12+2 phase power delivery design, which should allow you to perform a little overclocking, as well as a three-year limited warranty should anything happen to go wrong. The full-size ATX form factor allowed Asus to cram as many PCIe slots as possible and this board has two PCIe 4.0 x16 slots, followed by two additional PCIe 4.0 x1 slots.

For storage, you'll be able to utilize the array of SATA ports and two PCIe M.2 slots (one being PCIe 5.0, the other 4.0). Plenty of USB ports can be found on the rear, as well as the 2.5Gb networking, which is great for when upgrading your LAN further down the line. Four DIMM slots support a maximum of 128GB of DDR5 RAM, which is capable of hitting speeds up to 6400 MT/s. Even though we're looking at a lower-tier chipset and no PCIe 5.0 slots for GPUs and other expansion cards, Asus made sure to spend the necessary budget on some of the essentials.

The standard ATX form factor allowed Asus to space everything out for a cleaner aesthetic. There isn't a massive shroud covering most of the PCB and with everything being black, it's a stealthy-looking motherboard. It's not the most striking board in terms of design, but we've come to appreciate ASUS' more industrial look of its TUF products. If you're looking for one of the best-value AM5 motherboards for an AMD Ryzen 7000 PC build, consider giving the Asus TUF Gaming B650-PLUS a go.

Source: ASUS

The ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi is one of the best B550 motherboards you can buy for your AMD CPUs. It offers most of the features and capabilities of more expensive X570-chipset-based boards with an affordable price tag. It may not be the best motherboard for your AMD CPU, but it is what offers the most value for your money and is perfect for a budget build. The ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi features a 14-phase delivery, 2.5GB LAN, integrated WiFi 6, two M.2 slots, and more. The motherboard VRM is covered with a heatsink, and it comes with two EPS connectors.

You also get a ton of fan/pump headers, in addition to a bunch of RGB and USB headers on the motherboard. DDR4 support is present for up to 5100 for system memory, allowing you to match this board with a high-power AMD Ryzen 5000-series processor. Two full-sized PCIe slots are available with the primary one being reinforced for accepting GPUs. It provides 16 PCIe 4.0 lanes to the GPU, whereas the second slot runs at PCIe 3.0 x4 speeds. In terms of connectivity, you get a total of eight USB ports which include two USB 3.2 Gen 2, four USB 3.1 Gen1, and two USB 2.0. One of the USB 2.0 ports is designated for BIOS flash functionality.

In addition to the USB ports, you also get a single Intel 2.5 GbE LAN port, an HDMI, and DisplayPort, along with an audio stack consisting of 5-plug analog outputs plus SPDIF. The performance of the ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi is also on-point. It supports even the most demanding AMD CPU on the market. That being said, it's best to pair this particular motherboard with, say, an AMD Ryzen 5 5600X CPU for a mid-range build instead of a high-end rig. As such, it shouldn't have any issues running any 3000 or 5000 series CPU thanks to the AM4 socket and the B550 chipset. This motherboard is readily available on the market right now, but be sure to check out this link below to find the best price online.

Source: NZXT

Socket
AM5
Power Phases
16+2+1
PCI Slots
1x PCIe 5.0 x16, 2x PCIe 4.0 x16
Storage
4x SATA, 1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 2x PCIe 4.0 M.2
Audio
Realtek ALC1220
Networking
Realtek 2.5Gb, 2x2 Wi-Fi 6E (802.11 a/b/g/n/ac/ax)
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 3x USB-A 2.0
Rear I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 port, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, 4x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, 2x USB-A 2.0 ports, 1x HDMI 2.1, 5x Audio jacks, S/PDIF out
Pros
  • Gorgeous design
  • Great B650 performance
  • Plenty of connectivity
Cons
  • Shroud may block some 24-pin ATX connectors
$317 at Amazon $317 at Newegg $320 at NZXT

NZXT made a splash in the motherboard space with its N7 Z490 board for Intel builds, following which it quickly became a popular choice for a lot of people. The company quickly followed up with an AMD version and now we've got a board built for AMD's AM5 platform. The NZXT N7 B650E is our pick for the best motherboard design. It's stunning with a matte black or white shroud. The N7 B650E comes with PCIe 5.0 support, Wi-Fi 6E, four SATA ports, and a ton of USB ports for connecting all kinds of peripherals. It's one of the best-looking motherboards on this list, which looks best when paired with an NZXT PC case.

The NZXT N7 B650E motherboard comes in a black-and-white color scheme. It comes with a rather sophisticated heatsink/shroud covering almost the entirety of the motherboard. The only visible part of the PCB is next to the AM5 CPU socket, which will eventually get covered by the CPU heatsink. The heavily-shrouded design isn't for everyone, but it'll nicely blend into any and all kinds of PC builds without any issues. Those who don't like RGB lights on their motherboard will particularly like the N7 B550 since it doesn't have any RGB lights whatsoever. So if you're into RGB lighting, you'll have to look elsewhere.

The NZXT N7 B650E comes with two EPS connectors and a bunch of headers for your fan/pump, RGB, and USB ports. You also get two 4-pin NZXT RGB headers to control compatible NZXT products. You also get four DRAM slots on this board, although none of them are reinforced. It supports up to 128GB of RAM with listed speeds of up to DDR5-6000 (OC). The board has three PCIe slots in total out of which only the top slot is reinforced for the GPU. You also get three M.2 slots that accept drives up to 80mm long. NZXT attempted to get the most out of the B650 chipset, rivaling feature sets of more expensive boards.

Just like other premium motherboards in this collection, the NZXT N7 B650E also comes with a pre-installed I/O shield. You get ten USB ports in total out of which four are USB 3.2 Gen2 ports, four are USB 3.2 Gen1 ports, and the final two are USB 2.0 ports. You also get an HDMI port along with BIOS flashback and clear CMOS buttons and a Wi-Fi antenna. The performance of the N7 B650E is on-par with a lot of other premium motherboards on the market. It's a fantastic option for those looking at a clean motherboard that hides the PCB design. It's particularly good for builds involving NZXT PC cases or other NZXT components.

Source: ASUS
ASUS ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming WIFI
Best compact

Build a super-compact gaming PC with this board

$428 $470 Save $42

This compact ITX ASUS ROG Strix X670E motherboard has everything you'll need for a powerful PC that can fit inside a smaller form factor chassis.

Chipset
AMD X670
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
10+2
PCI Slots
1x PCIe 5.0 x16
Storage
2x SATA, 1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 1x PCIe 4.0 M.2
Audio
ALC4050
Networking
Intel 2.5Gb, Wi-Fi 6E
Internal I/O
1x USB 3.2-C Gen 2, 1x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1
Rear I/O
2x USB-C 4.0, 5x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2, 3x USB-A 2.0
Pros
  • Compact design
  • Good performance
Cons
  • Limited connectivity
  • Expensive
$428 at Amazon $470 at Newegg

This ASUS ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming WiFi motherboard is one to buy if you fancy building a small form factor PC. It's seriously small, rocking just one PCI slot. This size does mean you will lose some features, including only having one PCI slot, but you're able to create something special with a smaller footprint. ASUS managed to include a 10+2 phase power delivery design and the flagship X670E chipset. This means you should be able to run even the AMD Ryzen 9 without issues, though overclocking may produce some unstable results with the higher-tier processors. The single PCIe slot we mentioned earlier is 5.0, so too is one of two available PCIe M.2 slots for SSDs.

There's not a lot to the board itself, due to the size, but ASUS did a fine job in making it aesthetically pleasing and stealthy enough to fit inside more themed builds. There are a few headers for cooling and RGB lighting integration, as well as all the usual power connections. An added bonus of the ASUS ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming WiFi is the inclusion of the ROG Strix Hive. This external device hooks up to the motherboard and offers additional functionality, including an integrated DAC. This can prove useful if you position your compact PC somewhere that makes the rear I/O difficult to access.

For rear I/O, Asus managed to squeeze in two USB-C 4.0 ports, five USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, and a further three USB-A 2.0 ports, so even the most compact PC system will be able to handle a few gaming peripherals.

Source: MSI
MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE Motherboard
Best for enthusiasts

Try your luck at the overclocking leaderboard!

The MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE is an extreme motherboard with just about everything you could ask for in an enthusiastic platform. Designed for heavier use, MSI offers multiple reinforced PCIe 5.0 slots, four M.2 slots, robust power delivery with effective VRM cooling, and a striking dark board design.

Brand
MSI
Memory Slots
4
Memory Type
DDR5-5600
Form Factor
E-ATX
CPU Support
AMD Ryzen 7000+
Chipset
AMD X670
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
24+2+1
PCI Slots
3x PCIe 5.0 x16
Storage
4x M.2, 8x SATA
Audio
Realtek ALC4082 Codec, ESS ES9280AQ
Networking
10GbE, 2.5GbE, Wi-Fi 6E
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2, 1x USB-C 3.2 Gen2, 4x USB-A 3.2 Gen1, 4x USB-A 2.0
Rear I/O
2x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2, 7x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2
Pros
  • Impressive specifications
  • Excellent performance
  • Board design
Cons
  • Incredibly expensive
  • Overkill for most systems
$1262 at Amazon $1300 at Newegg

You likely won't require anything close to the MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE but it's designed for those with more advanced hardware and plans for some overclocking. Let's start with the impressive power setup, consisting of a 24+2+1 phase delivery design. The VRMs are covered by a large heatsink and MSI made good use of some thermal pads and MOSFET passive cooling. Audio is handled by the Realtek ALC4082 codec with help from the ESS ES9280AQ, which means movies, music, and (most importantly) games will sound amazing when paired with the best gaming headset.

For RAM, MSI states the board will happily support DDR5 modules with speeds up to 5600MT/s. The DIMM slots are conveniently located next to the migrated 4-pin EPS sockets. In fact, just about every major connection header on this motherboard has been adjusted to face perpendicular to the motherboard, instead of outward. This should aid in cable routing, especially for the thicker cabling. All three PCI slots are reinforced and support PCIe 5.0 for maximum bandwidth. If all that wasn't enough, there are four M.2 slots (one being PCIe 5.0), and 10GbE networking.

The rear I/O is just as ridiculous with seven USB-A 3.2 Gen2 ports, and two USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 ports. The MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE is incredibly expensive, almost triple the price of some of our recommendations here, but the price may be worth it if you require all these advanced features not typically found on lesser boards.

Source: ASRock
ASRock WRX80 Creator
Best workstation

For serious computing and performance

The ASRock WRX80 is a monster of a motherboard designed specifically for AMD Ryzen Threadripper 3000 and 5000 series processors. Looking to build a powerful workstation or server? Start your build right here.

Memory Slots
8
Memory Type
DDR4-4400
Form Factor
E-ATX
Wi-Fi
Yes
CPU Support
AMD Ryzen Threadripper PRO 3000/5000
Chipset
WRX80
Socket
sWRX
Power Phases
8
PCI Slots
7x PCIe 4.0
Storage
8x SATA, 2x M.2 SSD
Audio
Realtek ALC4050H+ALC1220
Networking
2x Marvell (Aquantia) 10Gb
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 1x USB 2.0
Rear I/O
2x Thunderbolt 4, 4x USB-C 3.2 Gen2, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen1
Pros
  • Amazing performance
  • Incredible bandwidth
  • Dual 10GbE networking
Cons
  • Expensive
  • Supports only Threadripper CPUs
$900 at Newegg $900 at Amazon

Last but not least, we've added the ASRock WRX80 Creator as our pick for the best motherboard for those looking to build a HEDT PC with an AMD Ryzen Threadripper processor. The ASRock WRX80 Creator is one of the most expensive motherboards you can buy, but that's because it comes with a long list of features that are not necessarily found on any of the mainstream motherboards we've listed above. It serves up to 64 lanes of PCIe at Gen4 speeds. It's also only the motherboard in this list that comes with a dual-fan heatsink covering its 8 105A voltage regulator phases.

The ASRock WRX80 Creator is an ATX motherboard that carries an AMD sWRX socket. There are two 40mm fans cooling the VRM, in addition to the regular 50mm fan that we usually see on other mainstream AMD motherboards. These VRM fans are enough to tame even the most powerful, fully-overclocked system without a hitch. There are two available M.2 PCIe 4.0 slots for SSDs, joining the seven PCIe 4.0 x16 slots for expansion cards.

The I/O shield comes pre-installed, which isn't all that surprising considering this is a high-end premium motherboard. You won't see any RGB lighting, however. The ASRock WRX80 Creator comes with 8 DIMM slots for impressive system memory capacity. It also goes without saying that you get plenty of headers for fan/pump, RGB lights, USB, and more. Overclocking is also in cards since this is a high-performance motherboard that's designed to push even the most demanding HEDT CPUs on the market.

Choosing the best AMD motherboard

It's not required to spend a fortune to enjoy the best of what's offered by AMD desktop CPUs, and we believe our collection here provides ample choice, covering multiple generations. We view the ASUS ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming as the best X670 chipset-based motherboard one can buy right now for a high-end PC build. It has a solid power phase delivery system for stable CPU overclocking, plenty of connectivity, and an excellent set of features ... for a price.

The ASUS TUF Gaming B650-PLUS is also a great option for those looking to buy an affordable AM5 motherboard. It has many of the same features offered by the X670E-E Gaming from ASUS but cuts a few to bring the price down. If you're not needing the absolute best platform for enthusiast overclocking and likely won't saturate all the available PCIe lanes offered by the B650 chipset, this motherboard will be more than enough.

Source: ASUS
Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming
Best overall

The Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming is a high-end Asus motherboard that supports PCIe 5.0 graphics, three PCIe 5.0 SSDs, and high-end Ryzen 7000 CPUs thanks to its large 18+2 stage VRM.

$480 at Amazon $480 at Newegg $480 at B&H
item.content:encoded:
Choosing the best motherboard for AMD processors usually involves picking one with an AM5 socket and chipset for the latest Ryzen chips. Depending on what you plan on using the system for, it's possible to spend anywhere between $100 and $1,000 on a motherboard for the best CPUs from AMD. This collection includes our favorite motherboard recommendations for AMD processors.

  • Source: ASUS
    Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming
    Best overall
    $480 at Amazon
  • GIGABYTE X670 AORUS Elite AX
    Runner-up
    $260 at Amazon
  • Source: ASUS
    Asus TUF Gaming B650-Plus
    Best value AM5
    $220 at Amazon
  • Source: ASUS
    ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi
    Best value AM4
    $181 at Amazon
  • Source: NZXT
    NZXT N7 B650E Gaming Wi-Fi 6 Motherboard
    Best design
    $317 at Amazon
  • Source: ASUS
    ASUS ROG Strix X670E-I Gaming WIFI
    Best compact
    $428 at Amazon
  • Source: MSI
    MSI MEG X670E GODLIKE Motherboard
    Best for enthusiasts
    $1262 at Amazon
  • Source: ASRock
    ASRock WRX80 Creator
    Best workstation
    $900 at Newegg

Our top picks for the best AMD motherboards in 2023

Source: ASUS
Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming
Best overall

The best AMD motherboard you can buy

The Asus ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming is a high-end Asus motherboard that supports PCIe 5.0 graphics, three PCIe 5.0 SSDs, and high-end Ryzen 7000 CPUs thanks to its large 18+2 stage VRM.

Memory Type
DDR5-6400+
Form Factor
ATX
Chipset
X670
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
18+2
PCI Slots
2x PCIe 5.0 x16, 1x PCie 4.0 x16
Storage
4x SATA, 2x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 2x PCIe 4.0 M.2
Audio
Realtek ALC4080
Networking
Intel 2.5Gb, 2x2 Wi-Fi 6E (802.11 a/b/g/n/ac/ax)
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2, 1x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 3x USB-A 2.0, 1x Thunderbolt header
Rear I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 port, 10x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, 2x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 ports
Pros
  • Excellent performance
  • Five M.2 SSD slots
  • Good overclocking support
Cons
  • Pricey
  • No 5Gb LAN
$480 at Amazon $480 at Newegg $480 at B&H

One of our favorite motherboards for AMD Ryzen 7000 processors is the ASUS ROG Strix X670E-E Gaming. It has almost everything you need for a stable PC build, even with the flagship AMD Ryzen 9 7950X. Let's start with the X670E chipset. It's an "extreme" version of the X670, reserved for more enthusiast-grade boards due to it being better for overclocking and having all the connectivity in the world. We've got an 18+2 phase power delivery design for the CPU and other nearby components, as well as two PCIe 5.0 x16 slots, one PCIe 4.0 x16 slot, three PCIe 5.0 M.2 SSD slots, and a single PCIe 4.0 M.2 SSD slot.

Source: XDA-Developers

This motherboard can work with the latest GPUs and storage, as well as many future products down the line. We're not using anywhere close to the limit of PCIe 5.0 for graphics cards, making the ASUS ROG Strix X670-E Gaming a good choice for the future. For USB ports on the rear I/O, ASUS made sure you have plenty of options, including 12 USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, one USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 port, and a further three more USB 2.0 ports. This is all rounded out with 2.5Gb networking for speedy access to your LAN and the outside world. It's simply one of the better X670E motherboards without spending a ridiculous amount of money.

GIGABYTE X670 AORUS Elite AX
Runner-up

Another great AM5 motherboard choice

$260 $280 Save $20

The Gigabyte X670 Aorus Elite AX has the essentials for a high-end gaming PC, including support for DDR5-6666 memory, four M.2 slots for SSDs (one of which supports PCIe 5.0), and a 16-stage VRM.

Memory Type
DDR5-6666+
Form Factor
ATX
Chipset
AMD X670
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
16+2+2
PCI Slots
1x PCIe 4.0 x16, 1x PCIe 4.0 x4, 1x PCIe 3.0 x1
Storage
1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 2x PCIe 4.0 M.2, 4x SATA
Networking
2.5 GbE, AMD Wi-Fi 6E RZ616
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB 2.0
Rear I/O
6x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2, 1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2, 4x USB-A 2.0
Pros
  • Great performance
  • Good overclock potential
  • Connectivity
Cons
  • No PCIe 5.0 slot
$260 at Amazon $280 at Newegg $280 at Best Buy

The Gigabyte X670 AORUS Elite AX is another top recommendation from us for AMD Ryzen 7000+ processors and the AM5 socket. Just like the Asus X670 motherboard we recommended above, this AORUS Elite AX board has plenty of premium features, including speedy networking, rapid M.2 storage slots, and decent power delivery for some overclocking. We're looking at the same standard ATX form factor, which means this motherboard will fit inside most PC cases. Beefy heatsinks cover the VRMs and with a 16+2+2 phase power delivery design, you shouldn't encounter any issues regarding heat.

As you'd expect from a premium motherboard, the M.2 slots are also covered with heatsinks/shroud, making it a great overall environment for cooling. Additionally, this motherboard sports a USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 port for the front panel. The rear I/O has numerous connection points, including 2.5GbE networking, six USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, two USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, one USB-C 3.2 Gen 2x2 port, and four USB-A 2.0 ports. You won't have any trouble hooking up all your gaming accessories to a system built with this motherboard. It also looks great and should fit in with most PC build themes.

The Gigabyte X670 AORUS Elite AX motherboard has two 8-pin connectors to feed power to the CPU. It goes without saying that you also get a ton of fan/pump, USB, and RGB headers on this motherboard. These headers are usually available in plenty of motherboards now, so it's nothing unusual. You also get up to four DRAM slots capable of running up to 128GB of DDR4 RAM with up to DDR5-6666+ speeds. Your mileage is bound to vary regarding speeds since not all memory modules have the same speeds. That being said, it's good to see the support for some of the fastest RAM kits available on the market.

The motherboard also comes with three PCIe slots. Two of those are full-length reinforced slots, whereas the last one is an x1 slot at the bottom without any reinforcement. We also like the fact that the I/O shield is pre-installed on this motherboard, which means that's one less step to worry about while building a PC. Overall, the Gigabyte X670 AORUS Elite AX is a fantastic motherboard that's capable of powering even the most demanding CPUs on the market. Not to mention, it also supports overclocking, so enthusiasts would love to have this board at their disposal for a fully decked-out AMD-based build.

Source: ASUS
Asus TUF Gaming B650-Plus
Best value AM5

Save some money and still build a powerful PC

The Asus TUF Gaming B650-Plus is a midrange motherboard with support for PCIe 5.0 SSDs and midrange to high-end Ryzen 7000 CPUs.

Form Factor
ATX
Chipset
AMD B650
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
12+2
PCI Slots
2x PCIe 4.0 x16, 2x PCIe 4.0 x1
Storage
4x SATA, 1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 2x PCIe 4.0 M.2
Networking
1x Realtek 2.5Gb
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 1, 1x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB-A 2.0
Rear I/O
2x USB 3.2 Gen 2, 4x USB 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB 2.0
Pros
  • Great value
  • DDR5 and PCIe 5.0 support
Cons
  • Only three PCIe M.2 slots
  • Limited rear I/O
$220 at Amazon $220 at Newegg $240 at Best Buy

Going with the latest AMD Ryzen processors doesn't mean you need to spend a fortune, even on a motherboard with an AM5 socket. The Asus TUF Gaming B650-PLUS rocks the AMD B650 chipset, which only loses a few features compared to the flagship X670 chipset, and we've seen motherboard vendors work some serious magic with this thing. We've got a 12+2 phase power delivery design, which should allow you to perform a little overclocking, as well as a three-year limited warranty should anything happen to go wrong. The full-size ATX form factor allowed Asus to cram as many PCIe slots as possible and this board has two PCIe 4.0 x16 slots, followed by two additional PCIe 4.0 x1 slots.

For storage, you'll be able to utilize the array of SATA ports and two PCIe M.2 slots (one being PCIe 5.0, the other 4.0). Plenty of USB ports can be found on the rear, as well as the 2.5Gb networking, which is great for when upgrading your LAN further down the line. Four DIMM slots support a maximum of 128GB of DDR5 RAM, which is capable of hitting speeds up to 6400 MT/s. Even though we're looking at a lower-tier chipset and no PCIe 5.0 slots for GPUs and other expansion cards, Asus made sure to spend the necessary budget on some of the essentials.

The standard ATX form factor allowed Asus to space everything out for a cleaner aesthetic. There isn't a massive shroud covering most of the PCB and with everything being black, it's a stealthy-looking motherboard. It's not the most striking board in terms of design, but we've come to appreciate ASUS' more industrial look of its TUF products. If you're looking for one of the best-value AM5 motherboards for an AMD Ryzen 7000 PC build, consider giving the Asus TUF Gaming B650-PLUS a go.

Source: ASUS
ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi
Best value AM4

Keep your old Ryzen CPU alive with this budget board

The Asus ROG Strix B550-F Gaming is one of the better B550 boards on the market, and it's a great option for those looking to build a powerful AMD-based build with one of the 5000-series processors.

Form Factor
ATX
Chipset
AMD B550
Socket
AM4
Power Phases
12+2
PCI Slots
1x PCIe 4.0 x16, 1x PCIe 3.0 x16, 3x PCIe 3.0 x1
Storage
6x SATA, 1x PCIe 4.0 M.2, 1x PCIe 3.0 M.2
Audio
S1220A
Networking
Intel I225-V 2.5Gb, Intel Wi-Fi 6 AX200
Internal I/O
4 x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB-C 3.2 Gen 1, 6x USB-A 2.0
Rear I/O
4x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 2x USB-C 3.2 Gen 1, 6x USB 2.0
Pros
  • Good value
  • Great performance
  • Connectivity
Cons
  • No DDR5 or PCIe 5.0 support
$181 at Amazon $190 at Newegg $181 at B&H

The ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi is one of the best B550 motherboards you can buy for your AMD CPUs. It offers most of the features and capabilities of more expensive X570-chipset-based boards with an affordable price tag. It may not be the best motherboard for your AMD CPU, but it is what offers the most value for your money and is perfect for a budget build. The ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi features a 14-phase delivery, 2.5GB LAN, integrated WiFi 6, two M.2 slots, and more. The motherboard VRM is covered with a heatsink, and it comes with two EPS connectors.

You also get a ton of fan/pump headers, in addition to a bunch of RGB and USB headers on the motherboard. DDR4 support is present for up to 5100 for system memory, allowing you to match this board with a high-power AMD Ryzen 5000-series processor. Two full-sized PCIe slots are available with the primary one being reinforced for accepting GPUs. It provides 16 PCIe 4.0 lanes to the GPU, whereas the second slot runs at PCIe 3.0 x4 speeds. In terms of connectivity, you get a total of eight USB ports which include two USB 3.2 Gen 2, four USB 3.1 Gen1, and two USB 2.0. One of the USB 2.0 ports is designated for BIOS flash functionality.

In addition to the USB ports, you also get a single Intel 2.5 GbE LAN port, an HDMI, and DisplayPort, along with an audio stack consisting of 5-plug analog outputs plus SPDIF. The performance of the ASUS ROG Strix B550-F Gaming Wi-Fi is also on-point. It supports even the most demanding AMD CPU on the market. That being said, it's best to pair this particular motherboard with, say, an AMD Ryzen 5 5600X CPU for a mid-range build instead of a high-end rig. As such, it shouldn't have any issues running any 3000 or 5000 series CPU thanks to the AM4 socket and the B550 chipset. This motherboard is readily available on the market right now, but be sure to check out this link below to find the best price online.

Source: NZXT
NZXT N7 B650E Gaming Wi-Fi 6 Motherboard
Best design

This is the board to buy for a showcase PC

stylish metal-clad motherboard for clean white builds.

Brand
NZXT
Memory Slots
4
Memory Type
DDR5-6000+
Form Factor
ATX
CPU Support
Ryzen 7000+
Chipset
B650
Socket
AM5
Power Phases
16+2+1
PCI Slots
1x PCIe 5.0 x16, 2x PCIe 4.0 x16
Storage
4x SATA, 1x PCIe 5.0 M.2, 2x PCIe 4.0 M.2
Audio
Realtek ALC1220
Networking
Realtek 2.5Gb, 2x2 Wi-Fi 6E (802.11 a/b/g/n/ac/ax)
Internal I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1, 3x USB-A 2.0
Rear I/O
1x USB-C 3.2 Gen 2 port, 2x USB-A 3.2 Gen 2 ports, 4x USB-A 3.2 Gen 1 ports, 2x USB-A 2.0 ports, 1x HDMI 2.1, 5x Audio jacks, S/PDIF out
Pros
  • Gorgeous design
  • Great B650 performance
  • Plenty of connectivity
Cons
  • Shroud may block some 24-pin ATX connectors
$317 at Amazon $317 at Newegg $320 at NZXT

NZXT made a splash in the motherboard space with its N7 Z490 board for Intel builds, following which it quickly became a popular choice for a lot of people. The company quickly followed up with an AMD version and now we've got a board built for AMD's AM5 platform. The NZXT N7 B650E is our pick for the best motherboard design. It's stunning with a matte black or white shroud. The N7 B650E comes with PCIe 5.0 support, Wi-Fi 6E, four SATA ports, and a ton of USB ports for connecting all kinds of peripherals. It's one of the best-looking motherboards on this list, which looks best when paired with an NZXT PC case.

The NZXT N7 B650E motherboard comes in a black-and-white color scheme. It comes with a rather sophisticated heatsink/shroud covering almost the entirety of the motherboard. The only visible part of the PCB is next to the AM5 CPU socket, which will eventually get covered by the CPU heatsink. The heavily-shrouded design isn't for everyone, but it'll nicely blend into any and all kinds of PC builds without any issues. Those who don't like RGB lights on their motherboard will particularly like the N7 B550 since it doesn't have any RGB lights whatsoever. So if you're into RGB lighting, you'll have to look elsewhere.

The NZXT N7 B650E comes with two EPS connectors and a bunch of headers for your fan/pump, RGB, and USB ports. You also get two 4-pin NZXT RGB headers to control compatible NZXT products. You also get four DRAM slots on this board, although none of them are reinforced. It supports up to 128GB of RAM with listed speeds of up to DDR5-6000 (OC). The board has three PCIe slots in total out of which only the top slot is reinforced for the GPU. You also get three M.2 slots that accept drives up to 80mm long. NZXT attempted to get the most out of the B650 chipset, rivaling feature sets of more expensive boards.

Just like other premium motherboards in this collection, the NZXT N7 B650E also comes with a pre-installed I/O shield. You get ten USB ports in total out of which four are USB 3.2 Gen2 ports, four are USB 3.2 Gen1 ports, and the final two are USB 2.0 ports. You also get an HDMI port along with BIOS flashback and clear CMOS buttons and a Wi-Fi antenna. The performance of the N7 B650E is on-par with a lot of other premium motherboards on the market. It's a fantastic option for those looking at a clean motherboard that hides the PCB design. It's particularly good for builds involving NZXT PC cases or other NZXT components.
